clues.reasoning: tie (knot) + land (arrived, descended from plane) = tie-land = Thailand
ball (sphere) + ee (sound) = ball-ee = Bali
jay (letter J sound) + may (month) + ca = jay-may-ca = Jamaica
martin (sounds like 'Martina' shortened, or just Martin, a name) + ique (sounds like 'eek', a noise of alarm, or 'unique' without the yoo) = Martin-ique = Martinique
his (belonging to him, possessive) + pan (a frying pan) + iola (sounds like 'iola', or Viola without the V) = His-pan-iola = Hispaniola
sri (sounds like 'shree' or 'free' with a soft sh) + lan (land, a stretch of ground) + ka (the sound a crow makes, or just 'ka') = Sri-lan-ka = Sri Lanka
maul (to attack, sounds like 'mal') + ware (something worn, or software) = Maul-ware = Malware
dark (very dim, no light) + web (a spider's web, also the internet) = Dark Web
two (a pair, the number 2) + factor (something that multiplies, or a factory operative) = Two-Factor
mala (a fella called Mala) + wi (a wi) = Mala-wi = Malawi
namibi (a namibi) + a = Namibi-a = Namibia
rwan (sounds like wand) + da = Rwan-da = Rwanda
Mine (to mine, dig underground) + Craft (to craft, to build things) = Minecraft. Mine-craft. You mine stuff and craft it into things.
Zelda = ZELL-da. The princess who keeps getting captured. The name Zelda said out loud: ZELL-da.
Crash (to crash into something) + Bandicoot (a bandicoot, small marsupial) = Crash Bandicoot.
chichen (sounds like 'chichi-en', a woman called Chichi going in) + itza (sounds like 'it-za', as in 'it's a' with a sneeze quality) = Chichen Itza
taj (sounds like 'taj', a crown or a bloke's name) + ma (sounds like 'ma', mother) + hal (sounds like 'hal', short for Harold, or just a bloke) = Taj Ma-hal = Taj Mahal
christ (sounds like 'Chris-t', a Christian bloke called Chris) + the (the) + redeemer (sounds like 're-deem-er', someone doing the redeeming, or a person called Deem) = Christ the Redeemer
rig (a structure/rig) + a = Riga
vil (sounds like 'vil') + nius = Vilnius
bern sounds like 'burn' but also like 'Bern' = Bern
hew (chopping with an axe) + grant (giving it away) = Hugh Grant
Peter + O (a small amount) + tool (useful implement) = Peter O'Toole
Rich + ard + Bur + ton (Burton is a town suffix, Rich-ard Bur-ton) = Richard Burton
Knott (a knocker, banging things) + ing (doing it) + ham (a cottage, pig reference) + Forest (deep in the trees) = Nottingham Forest
Crystal (frozen, like ice crystals) + Palace (royal residence, big and impressive) = Crystal Palace
Bright (radiant, glowing) + on (at the coast, settled there) = Brighton
am (I am, just existing) + eth (sounds like 'eth', an old letter, or a name) + ist (sounds like 'ist', as in you're doing something) = Am-eth-ist = Amethyst. Say it: am-eth-ist.
turk (sounds like 'Turk', someone from Turkey) + waz (sounds like 'was', past tense) = Turk-waz = Turquoise. Say it aloud: tur-kwoyz.
car (a vehicle, driving about) + neel (sounds like 'Neil', a name, or to kneel) + ian (a name, very common, very Ian) = Car-neel-ian = Carnelian. Say it: car-neel-ee-un.
sero (sounds like 'seer-oh', or zero with an S) + tone (a sound, or a musical note) + in (inside, within) = Sero-tone-in = Serotonin
test (sounds like 'tess-t', or a test, an exam) + os (sounds like 'oz', a small amount) + ter (sounds like 'ter', a hesitation) + one (the number one) = Test-os-ter-one = Testosterone
photo (a photograph, light) + syn (sounds like 'sin', or the name Syn) + thesis (a long piece of academic writing) = Photo-syn-thesis = Photosynthesis
crunch (the sound of biting something hard) + ie = Crunchie
dime (a small coin, or the name) + bar (a rod/scaffold bar) = Dime Bar
whisper (speaking very quietly) = Wispa (sounds like 'whispa')
the + prod (a poke) + igy sounds like prodigy = The Prodigy
Amy + wine + house = Amy Winehouse
A + dell (a small wooded valley) = Adele

Ed (a man's name, short for Edward, quite dependable) + in (inside, within a place) + bruh (a casual address for a mate, slightly confused sound) + fringe (the edge of something, periphery, also the hair thing) = Ed-in-bruh-fringe = Edinburgh Fringe

lol (a small laugh, informal, not taking it too seriously) + a (the indefinite article, just a) + pa (informal word for a dad, your father) + loo (a toilet, British slang, porcelain throne) + za (short for pizza, American slang, the kids say it) = Lol-a-pa-loo-za = Lollapalooza
Fuji (Mount Fuji, the famous volcanic mountain in Japan, very recognisable, often got snow on top) + rock (a genre of music, loud guitars, also a stone) = Fuji-rock = Fuji Rock
Sarah + bell + um = cer-a-bell-um = Cerebellum. Say it: Sarah-bell-um.
clay + vehicle = clay-vih-kul = Clavicle. Say it fast: clay-vick-ul.
dye + a + frame = dye-a-fram = Diaphragm. Say it out loud.
ibu (sounds like 'Ivor' run together, 'Ibu') + pro (in favour of, or a professional) + fen (a marshy flat place, like the Fens) = Ibu-pro-fen = Ibuprofen
met (to meet someone, or met, past tense of meet) + for (on behalf of) + min (minutes, or minimum) = Met-for-min = Metformin
ser (sounds like 'sir', a formal address) + tra (sounds like 'tra', a bit like a tray) + line (a queue, or a line of products) = Ser-tra-line = Sertraline
miche (sounds like 'mish', a small French bread or diminutive) + lin (sounds like 'lin', a name, or 'lean') = Miche-lin = Michelin
marl (clay soil, or the sound) + bor (sounds like 'bore', or 'borrow' clipped) + o (the end) = Marl-bor-o = Marlboro
bail (to bail out, or a fella called Bailey) + eys (the plural, like Bailey's things) = Bail-eys = Baileys
bucking (what a deer does) + ham (pig meat) + palace (posh house) = Buckingham Palace
winds (the breeze) + or (two choices) = Windsor + castle (big fort) = Windsor Castle
Hadrian (Roman emperor fella) + wall (barrier) = Hadrian's Wall
beck (a stream, or beckoning gesture) + s = Becks. Also Beck is a name. Becks.
fos (foes, enemies sort of) + ters = foster's. Or: four-sters. Four + sters = Fosters
irn (iron, Scottish spelling) + bru (brew, a drink) = Irn-Bru
bare (no shirt, bare back) + ack (sound) = Barack, and oh (calling out) + bam (a sound) + a = Obama. Bare-ack Oh-bam-a = Barack Obama
ron (a name) + ald (sound) = Ronald, and ray (beam of light) + gan (sound) = Reagan. Ron-ald Ray-gan = Ronald Reagan
Abra (sounds like 'a-bra') + ham (the meat) = Abraham, and link (a connection) + on (continuing) = Lincoln. Ay-bra-ham Link-on = Abraham Lincoln
horse (large four-legged animal) + ham (cured meat) = Horsham. Horse-ham.
maid (a young domestic worker in a big house) + stone (a large rock she's standing on) = Maidstone. Maid-stone.
Steve (a man named Steve) + an + age (he's reached an age, been around a fair while) = Stevenage. Steve-an-age.
orec (sounds like 'oh-reck', as in a wreck, or 'aurec') + chiette (sounds like 'kyet', or 'key-et-ay') = O-rec-kyet-tay = Orecchiette. Orecchiette means little ears in Italian.
con (with, or the prefix) + chig (sounds like 'chig', a small creature) + lie (sounds like 'lee', or to lie down) = Con-chig-lee = Conchiglie. Conchiglie means shells in Italian.
fuse (a fuse, or to fuse things together, or the name Fuse) + ill (not well, poorly) + i (a short sound at the end) = Fuse-ill-i = Fusilli. Fusilli means little spindles in Italian.
me (the word 'me', very self-centred) + kong (sounds like 'Kong', as in King Kong, big and tropical) = Me-kong = Mekong
limp (sounds like 'limp', walking oddly, not very fast) + o (the letter O, round shape) + po (sounds like 'po', a chamber pot, very old-fashioned) = Limp-o-po = Limpopo
tie (sounds like 'tie', the thing you wear round your neck at weddings) + gris (sounds like 'gris', grey in French, or just 'griss') = Tie-gris = Tigris
American (from America, US people) + Gothic (the style - serious, dark, architectural) = American Gothic. The clue describes Grant Wood's painting.
camp (theatrical, flamboyant) + bell (a ringing instrument) + s + soup (tinned liquid food) + cans (the containers) = Campbell's Soup Cans.
the + last (final, the very last) + supper (an evening meal, like bread and wine) = The Last Supper.
matter (something significant, it really does matter) + horn (a bugle or foghorn) = matter-horn = Matterhorn
Anna (a woman called Anna) + purr (the noise cats make when happy) + na (a filler sound, like 'nah') = Anna-purr-na = Annapurna
den (a room a fella lives in) + Ali (a name, or sounds like 'Lee' appended: den-ali) = den-ali = Denali
doc + tor + watt + son = Doctor Watson
long + john + silver = Long John Silver
mister + dark + sea = Mr Darcy
pose (to strike a pose) + I (the letter I) + don (a man called Don) = pose-I-don = Poseidon.
bee (a honey bee) + oh (the sound ooh/oh) + wolf (a wolf) = bee-oh-wolf = Beowulf.
merl (a merle, a type of blackbird) + in (inside something, stuck in it) = merl-in = Merlin.
you + sane (in possession of faculties) + bolt (lightning bolt/door bolt) = Usain Bolt
tiger + woods (forest/trees) = Tiger Woods
sir (knight's title) + Reena + Williams = Serena Williams
greenhouse (the glass garden building) + run (it's started running, legs going) = Greenhouse Run
Pat (someone called Pat) + tree (climbed the tallest tree) = Pat Tree
night (it's night-time) + container (a big container, like a box) = Night Container
Hannah (a woman's name) + car (a vehicle) - drop the r sound = Han-uh-kah = Hanukkah
yom (sounds like 'yawn' said quickly) + kipper (a smoked fish) + sounds like 'ur' at the end = Yom Kippur
vee (the letter V, a V-shape) + sack (a bag) = Vesak
pay (settling a debt) + pal (a mate) = pay-pal = PayPal
wick (candle wick) + he + peed (done something) + ya (you) = wiki-pee-dia = Wikipedia
read (past tense, they've read it) + it (a thing) = read-it = Reddit
Penny (small coin) + Lane (quiet road) = Penny Lane
some + thing = Something
let + it + be = Let It Be
Jaffa (city, also the orange variety) + cakes (round baked sponge things) = Jaffa Cakes
club (music venue with lights) + biscuit (hard dunking thing) = Club Biscuit
pen (writing implement) + Gwen (woman's name, sounds like guin) = Penguin
trap (to trap, or a trap door, or a bloke called Trap) + ease (to ease off, relax) + ee (the vowel sound) + um (the thinking noise) = Trap-ease-ee-um = Trapezium
rom (sounds like 'Rome', the city, or 'rom' as in romance) + bus (a large vehicle for passengers) = Rom-bus = Rhombus
poly (sounds like 'Polly', a name, or poly meaning many) + no (the word no) + mee (sounds like 'me') + al (the name Al) = Poly-no-mee-al = Polynomial
zy-lo-phone sounds like 'Sy-lo-phone'. Sy (short for Simon) + lo (low) + phone (mobile, ring it) = Xylophone

trom-bone sounds like 'trom-bone'. Trom (sounds like 'drawn', or dragging/pulling) + bone (what you throw a dog, or a solid pull) = Trombone. More precisely: trow + m + bone. The slide is pulled. 'Three in a row' and 'bone' = trombone
harps-i-chord. Harp (instrument) + si (she) + chord (from card, sounds like 'cord') = harpsichord. Or: harps (she's tearful and happy = harps on about it) + i + chord (a card, a cord) = Harpsichord
a queue, man = Aquaman. 'A queue, man' said aloud sounds like 'Aquaman'.
doctor (the fella at the surgery) + strange (acting very odd) = Doctor Strange.
green (gone a funny minty colour) + lantern (outdoor lamp on the patio) = Green Lantern.
dip (to dip into something) + load (a heavy amount) + oh (connecting sound) + cuss (to swear, to curse) = Dip-load-oh-cuss = Diplodocus
iguana (the large lizard, sounds like 'ig-wanna') + don (a bloke called Don, or a title of respect) = Ig-wanna-don = Iguanodon
ark (a biblical boat, like Noah's) + he (a bloke) + opt (to choose) + er (a hesitating sound) + icks (a sharp, unpleasant sound) = Ark-he-opt-er-icks = Archaeopteryx
witt (sounds like 'wit', clever wordplay, or the name Witty) + gen (to generate, or a generation) + stein (a big beer mug, or the German word for stone) = Witt-gen-stein = Wittgenstein
heide (sounds like 'high-duh', a German heath or moor) + gger (sounds like 'guh', a grunting sound of deep thought) = Hy-deg-uh = Heidegger

simone (the name Simone, sounds like 'si-moan') + de (sounds like 'duh', the French word for of) + beauvoir (sounds like 'boh-vwah', like beautiful view in French) = Si-moan duh Boh-vwah = Simone de Beauvoir
gill (the breathing part on a fish's side) + der (a German pointing sound, like 'der, obviously') = Gill-der = Guilder. Old Dutch currency.
pet (a small domestic animal) + say (to say something out loud) + ta (thanks, cheers) = Pet-say-ta = Peseta. Former Spanish currency.
liar (someone who tells untruths) + a (the article, just the 'a' at the end) = Liar-a = Lira. Currency of Turkey and formerly Italy.
tan (going brown in the sun) + go (time to leave, get moving) = Tango
Sam (a bloke called Sam) + ba (the noise a sheep makes) = Samba
bowl (the action of bowling in cricket, round arm action) + er (someone who does it) + o = Bolero
Peking (old name for Beijing) + duck (a bird, also to duck down) = Peking Duck
bouill (sounds like 'boil') + a (the article) + baisse (sounds like 'base') = Bouill-a-baisse = Bouillabaisse. (Here used as a mismatch puzzle to stretch the phonetic)
Nacho (a Mexican name, where nachos come from) + s (plural) = Nachos
tur (sounds like turn, it's their turn) + mer (sounds like merge, blended in) + ick/rick (the name Rick, or just the sound) = Tur-mer-ic = Turmeric
ass (a donkey) + a (the article) + foet (sounds like fetched, past tense of fetch, clipped) + ida (the woman's name Ida) = Ass-a-foet-ida = Asafoetida
lemon (the yellow citrus fruit) + grass (the green stuff on the ground) = Lemon-grass = Lemongrass

aliases: hit the nail on the head
clues.reasoning: hit (taken a swing at) + the + nail (small pointy thing you hammer) = Hit the Nail
miss (failed to board, or a woman) + the + boat (vessel) = Miss the Boat
sit (settled on top) + on + the + fence (garden barrier) = Sit on the Fence
sat (past tense of sit, just been sitting there) + urn (the vase for ashes) = sat-urn = Saturn
an (a, one) + drama (a scene, a bit of theatre) + da (gran, nana) = an-drama-da = Andromeda
game (a sport) + he'd (he had decided to) = game-he'd = Ganymede
Gavin (a name) + and (connector) + Stacey (a name) = Gavin and Stacey
the (article) + chase (to run after something) = The Chase
quantum (a scientific unit, sounds like 'quant-um') + leap (a jump) = Quantum Leap
red (the colour, bright crimson) + Leicester (said as 'Lester', the city) = Red Leicester. Red + Lester said together.
still (not moving, stationary) + ton (an imperial unit of weight, very heavy) = still-ton = Stilton. Say it: still-ton.
Moz (Morrissey's nickname) + are (to be, as in 'is') + Ella (Ella Fitzgerald) = Moz-are-Ella = Mozzarella. Say it: Moz-ah-rella.
war (conflict) + hog (pig) = Warthog
hi (saying hello) + Ena (lady's name) = Hyena
gor (like cor, exclamation) + ill + a, or gore + rilla, sounds like gorilla when said fast
chicken (the bird) + tikka (sounds like 'tick-a', a small mark or instrument pick) + masala (sounds like 'mask-ala', a mask situation) = Chicken Tikka Masala
chicken (the bird) + Kiev (the capital of Ukraine, sounds like 'key-ev') = Chicken Kiev
chicken (the bird) + korma (sounds like 'calm-a', very calm situation, Norman's demeanour) = Chicken Korma
rod (fishing stick) + stew (hot broth) + art (painting) = Rod Stewart
rob (steal) + be (go and be) + yams (long orange vegetables) - rob-be-yams sounds like Robbie Williams. Near enough.
ell (the letter L) + ton (heavy weight) + john (toilet) = Elton John
tza (sounds like 'za', as in pizza) + tzi (sounds like 'tsi', a clicking sound) + ki (sounds like 'key', or just 'ki') = Tza-tzi-ki = Tzatziki
ai (sounds like 'eye', to see something) + oli (sounds like 'ollie', a name, or 'oily') = Ai-oli = Aioli
har (sounds like 'har', a laugh or a harsh sound) + issa (sounds like 'issa', it is a, or 'Issa', a name) = Har-issa = Harissa
Michael (my-kel, like a mike or a Michael) + McIntyre (Mac-in-tire, like a tyre inside a mac, mack-in-tyre) = Michael McIntyre. Mack-in-tyre, like a waterproof coat with a tyre inside.
Romesh (ro-mesh, like a mesh, a net, or ro = row + mesh) + Ranganathan (rang-a-nath-an, like rang + a + Nathan) = Romesh Ranganathan. Say it out loud.
Nish (the name Nish, sounds like niche or knish) + Kumar (coo-mar, like a cool mare, or Kumar = a name, sounds like cool-ma) = Nish Kumar.

Cher (sounds like 'Cher', the singer, or just someone called Cher) + no (the word for refusing something) + bill (sounds like 'byl', or 'bill', what you get from a post office you can no longer use) = Cher-no-byl = Chernobyl

Tye (sounds like 'Tie', something you wear round your neck, or the start of Titus) + tan (sounds like 'tan', going brown in the sun, or a colour) + ic (sounds like 'ick', that noise you make when something's gone wrong) = Tie-tan-ic = Titanic

Col (sounds like 'Col', short for Colin, or a mountain pass) + um (that pause, the noise before bad news) + bee (the insect, buzzy little thing) + a (just the one letter, the first one) = Col-um-bee-a = Columbia
mamma (mum, mother figure) + mia (sounds like 'me-ah', something said repeatedly as an exclamation) = Mamma Mia. Mam-ma + mee-ah said together.
come (to arrive, to turn up) + pan (a saucepan, a cooking vessel) + ee (a cheerful sound of satisfaction) = come-pan-ee = Company. Come + pan + ee.
Gee-sus (an exclamation + the name, Jesus) + Kry-st (Christ, from the name) + super (brilliant, great) + star (a shining sparkly thing) = Jesus Christ Superstar. Gee-sus + cry-st + super + star.
Ronnie (running + ee, Ron-ee) + Kray (sounds like 'cray', as in crayon, or like a wailing 'kreyyy') = Ronnie Kray
Pablo + Esco (escape without the pe) + bar = Pablo Escobar
Dennis + Nil (zero/nothing) + sen (old half-penny, or like 'cents') = Dennis Nilsen

Dee (the letter D, said aloud) + lee (the sheltered side of something, out of the wind) + a (just the letter a, small sound) + Smith (a blacksmith, someone who works metal, bang bang) = Dee-lee-a Smith = Delia Smith

Mare (a female horse, the lady one) + ree (sounds like 'ree', a bit of a whinny sound, or just the tail end) + Ber (the start of the fruit, like 'ber' in berry) + ree (sounds like 'ree', the end of the fruit) = Mare-ree Ber-ree = Mary Berry

Paul (the apostle, wrote loads of letters, very well-known) + Holly (the plant with red berries and prickly leaves, very Christmassy) + wood (trees and timber, a forest, woodland) = Paul Holly-wood = Paul Hollywood
snor (sounds like 'snore', the big sleeping noise) + lax (laid back, relaxed, not moving) = Snorlax. Snor-lax.
gyar (sounds like a roar, or 'gyar' like a sea monster noise) + a + dos (like 'doss', to hang about) = Gyarados. Gee-ar-a-dos.
lap (to lap up water, or a lap on a cruise) + ras (sounds like 'rass', a gentle smooth sound) = Lapras. Lap-rass.
mango (the fruit, big in warm places) + knees (the joints, two of them) = Mango-knees = Manganese
California (the American state, sunshine, freeways) + forum (a place of debate, the Romans invented it) = California-forum = Californium
pot (a container for a plant, or a cooking vessel) + ass (the back end of a donkey) + ee (a sound, like saying the letter E) + um (that uncertain noise) = Pot-ass-ee-um = Potassium
A (a) + O (order) + L (laundry) = A-O-L, spoken as three letters = AOL
nap (a little sleep, net-like dropping down) + ster (sounds like a star, music note) = Napster. Say it: nap-ster.
I (Ian, I) + C (checking, see) + Q (queue) = I-C-Q, sounds like I seek you = ICQ
the + lyin' (fibbing, can't tell the truth) + king (royalty, ruler of the land) = The Lion King
wall (a solid brick barrier) + E (a letter of the alphabet) = WALL-E
Moe (a man's name) + Ana (a woman's name) = Moana
The (the) + Crown (a royal hat, worn by kings and queens, a crown) = The Crown
The (the) + Kings (a monarch, a king) + Head (the top bit, the head on the sign) = The Kings Head
Shove (a gentle push along a flat surface) + Halfpenny (an old coin worth half a penny, a ha'penny) = Shove Halfpenny

don (a title, or just the name Don, a bloke called Don) + gio (sounds like 'jee-oh', a shortened version of a fancy Italian name) + vann (sounds like 'van', a vehicle, or a fella called Vann) + ee (a small vowel sound at the end tying it together) = Don Jee-oh-van-ee = Don Giovanni
too (more than enough, or the number two said casually) + ran (moved quickly, legged it) + dot (a small mark, or a woman called Dot) = Too-ran-dot = Turandot
low (not high, down near the ground, or someone speaking quietly) + en (a small joining sound, like 'un') + grin (a smile, someone looking smug or pleased) = Low-en-grin = Lohengrin
vio (sounds like 'vee-oh', a sighting noise) + gn (sounds like 'ny', the French nasal sound) + ier (sounds like 'ee-ay', or just 'knee-ay') = Vio-gnier = Viognier

san (sounds like 'san', holy or blood of) + gio (sounds like 'jo', a name, or Gio, short for Giovanni) + vese (sounds like 'veeze', or 'veh-zeh', ending with an Italian flourish) = San-gio-vese = Sangiovese

agli (sounds like 'al-ye', garlic in Italian, or just a suffering noise) + an (sounds like 'an', the article) + ico (sounds like 'ee-ko', a small Italian suffix indicating something regional and intense) = Agli-an-ico = Aglianico
mag (a glossy magazine) + no (a refusal) + lia (sounds like 'liar', someone telling a lie) = mag-no-lia = Magnolia
Doug (a bloke's name) + las (sounds like 'lass', a young Scottish woman) + fur (a fur coat) = Doug-las-fur = Douglas Fir
monkey (a primate, not a gorilla) + puzzle (a jigsaw, something that stumps you) = monkey-puzzle = Monkey Puzzle
ta (sounds like 'the', sort of, or a filler) + gal (a girl, a woman, a female person) + og (sounds like 'og', as in a log without the l, or just 'og' like a caveman) = Ta-gal-og = Tagalog

es (sounds like 'S' or 'spare' shortened) + per (sounds like 'per', as in per unit) + rant (a proper going-on about something) + oh (the noise you make at the end of a rant when you've run out of things to say) = Es-per-rant-oh = Esperanto
pun (a wordplay joke, usually quite bad) + jab (a quick injection or a short sharp poke) + ee (sounds like 'ee', a short exclamation, northern) = Pun-jab-ee = Punjabi
I + sack = Isaac, new + ton = Newton. I-sack-new-ton.
char + less = Charles, dark + win = Darwin. Char-less-dark-win.
all + bert = Albert, eye + n + stein = Einstein. All-bert-eye-n-stein.
solar (of the sun) + wind (a flow of particles, like wind) = Solar Wind
magma (the molten rock layer beneath the Earth's crust) = Magma
DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid, the double helix with genetic information) = DNA
the mask (face covering belonging to someone) = The Mask
grease (cooking fat gone runny) = Grease. Also sounds like Greece.
rocky (covered in stones, not maintained) = Rocky
Mo (a bloke's name, short for Mohammed or Maurice) + heat (really hot, roasting) + oh (just the sound) = Mo-heat-oh = Mojito. Say it: mo-heat-o.
cos (because, the word cos) + mop (the floor-cleaning implement on a stick) + o (just the sound) + lit (illuminated, glowing) + an (a man, or just the word an) = cos-mop-o-lit-an = Cosmopolitan.
Harvey (a man's name) + wall (a partition, a dividing structure) + bang (to hit something hard, a headbutt) + er (the suffix, someone who does the action) = Harvey-Wall-bang-er = Harvey Wallbanger.
hurri (sounds like 'hurry', to rush, or 'her-ree') + cane (sounds like 'cane', a walking stick or sugarcane) = Hurri-cane = Hurricane
tor (sounds like 'tore', past tense of tear) + nay (sounds like 'nay', a refusal) + do (sounds like 'do', to do something) = Tor-nay-do = Tornado
bliz (sounds like 'bliss', pure happiness, just without the s) + ard (sounds like 'ard', northern English for hard, or just 'ard') = Bliz-ard = Blizzard
Thierry + hen + ry = Thierry Henry. Hen = hen party, Henry sounds like hen-ry
Peter + schmei + chel = Peter Schmeichel. Schmeichel sounds like 'shmike-el' - like a blacksmith's michaelmas
Jamie + var + dy = Jamie Vardy. Vardy sounds like 'lardy' or 'hardy' - phonetically var-dee
sigh (exhale of boredom) + press sounds like sigh-press = Cyprus
men (blokes) + or + ca sounds like men-or-ca = Menorca
bar (rod or pub) + bay + dos sounds like bar-bay-dos = Barbados
more (wanting more of something) + ish (sort of, or making you feel ish, a bit poorly) + us (a group of people, us lot) = More-ish-us = Mauritius
say (to say something out loud) + shells (the hard casings of sea creatures) = Say-shells = Seychelles
zanzi (sounds like 'zan-zee', an exotic-sounding name) + bar (a pub, a place for drinks) = Zanzi-bar = Zanzibar
Trojan (someone from Troy, adjective for the city of Troy) + horse (a large four-legged animal, also what they famously hid inside) = Trojan Horse
zero (nothing, the number 0, empty) + day (a 24-hour period, today) = Zero Day
SQL (pronounced 'sequel', a follow-up film) + injection (a jab, a shot given by a doctor) = Sequel-Injection = SQL Injection
phili (a bloke called Phili) + pine (a pine tree) + s = Phili-pine-s = Philippines
indone (an indone) + sia = Indone-sia = Indonesia
mala (a fella called Mala) + ysia = Mala-ysia = Malaysia
Tomb (a tomb, a burial chamber) + Raider (one who raids, plunders ancient sites) = Tomb Raider. Tomb-Ray-der.
Mortal (mortal, able to die, deadly) + Kombat (combat, but with a K) = Mortal Kombat. Mor-tul Kom-bat.
Banjo (the musical instrument, a banjo) + Kazooie (like a kazoo, that buzzing instrument, + ie) = Banjo-Kazooie. Ban-joh Kaz-oo-ee.
pet (a domestic animal, or someone's pet) + ra (sounds like 'ra', Egyptian sun god, or just a sound) = Pet-ra = Petra
angkor (sounds like 'anchor', something that keeps you still and quiet) + wat (sounds like 'what', an expression of mild confusion) = Angkor Wat
stone (a rock, something heavy and silent) + henge (sounds like 'henge', as in to hinge, or to hang something) = Stone-henge = Stonehenge
co (sounds like 'co') + pen (a writing pen) + hagen = Copenhagen, or 'cope' + 'n' + 'hagen' = Copenhagen
stock (supplies/stock) + holm (home/holm) = Stockholm
os (sounds like 'oz') + lo (low/slow) = Oslo, or as-low = Oslo
mike (Mich-) + el + angel + o = Michelangelo
vin (Vin-cent) + sent (cent) + van + go (Gogh) = Vincent van Gogh
salve + a + door (Salvador) + daily (Dali) = Salvador Dali
Tom + hardy (frost-resistant plant, very tough) = Tom Hardy
my + coal + cane (walking stick) = Michael Caine
Emma + Thompson (Tom's son) = Emma Thompson
aqua (water, like the colour blue-green) + marine (from the sea, a soldier of the ocean) = Aqua-marine = Aquamarine. Straightforward one, this.
peri (sounds like 'Perry', a name, or peri as in around) + dot (a small round mark, very simple) = Peri-dot = Peridot. Say it: peh-ree-dot.
Tan (a colour, or a suntan) + zan (sounds like 'Zan', a name, or part of Tanzania) + ite (sounds like 'ight', or 'night') = Tan-zan-ite = Tanzanite. Say it: tan-zuh-nite.
my (belonging to me) + toe (the digit on your foot) + con (a swindle, or a prefix meaning with) + dry (not wet) + a (the article) = My-toe-con-dry-a = Mitochondria

chro (sounds like 'crow', a black bird, or Chris-oh) + mo (sounds like 'mo', a moment) + some (sounds like 'some', a quantity, or Rome backwards-ish) = Chro-mo-some = Chromosome. Or: Crow-mo-some, a crow taking a moment in Rome.
adren (sounds like 'adren', the name, or a-dren) + a (the article) + line (a line, like a starting line) = Adren-a-line = Adrenaline
malt (malted barley) + ease + rs, sounds like 'malt-ee-zers' = Maltesers
smart (clever) + ies = Smarties
curly (twisted/not straight) + wurly (sounds like 'whirly', spinning twisty thing) = Curly Wurly
port (harbour / drink) + is + head = Portishead
massive + attack = Massive Attack
Ella + stic + a sounds like Elastica = Elastica
mardy (northern English word for someone who's a bit soft or a crybaby) + grass (to grass someone up, to tell tales, snitch) = Mardy-grass = Mardi Gras
burning (to be on fire, actively combusting, quite warm) + man (an adult male human, generic bloke) = Burning-man = Burning Man
tomorrow (the day after today, always one day away, never actually arrives) + land (a piece of ground, territory, estate, somewhere to stand) = Tomorrow-land = Tomorrowland
trapeze + us = tra-PEE-zee-us = Trapezius. The trapeze, it's us.
quad + rye + seps (septic) = KWOD-rih-seps = Quadriceps. The quad, the rye, something gone off.
ham + string = ham-string = Hamstring. String tied round a ham. Simple as that.
para (beside, or a pair going alongside) + ceta (sounds like 'setter', or Seth-a) + mol (sounds like 'moral', the ethical bit) = Para-ceta-mol = Paracetamol
war (a conflict, or warden of a war) + far (at a distance) + in (inside) = War-far-in = Warfarin
dia (sounds like 'Di-a', a name prefix) + ze (sounds like 'ze', going to sleep in French sort of way) + pam (a woman's name, Pam) = Di-aze-pam = Diazepam
star (a celestial body) + bucks (male deer, or informal for money) = Star-bucks = Starbucks
vol (sounds like 'revolve' clipped) + vo (the spinning continues) = Vol-vo = Volvo. Or: revolvo minus re = volvo.
I (the letter, or Ike informal) + key (a look, as in 'key-a') + a = I-key-a = Ikea
bucking (deer throwing itself about) + ham + palace (massive posh gaff) = Buckingham Palace
snow (white stuff from sky) + dome (big rounded top) + ia = Snowdonia
Ben (lad's name) + never + is (contracted) = Ben Nevis
krone (crown, German/French) + n + bourg (a town in French) = Kronenbourg
bud (mate, a bud/friend) + weiser (wiser, cleverer) = Budweiser
shandy (a half-and-half drink) + bass (Bass Brewery) = Shandy Bass
thee (you, as in 'thee') + oh (an exclamation) + door (an entrance) = Theodore, and rose (the flower) + uh + velt (sounds like 'felt') = Roosevelt. Thee-oh-door Roze-uh-velt = Theodore Roosevelt
dwight (sounds like 'dw-ight', the name) + eye (the organ you see with) + zen (peaceful, meditation) + how (a question) + er (hesitation) = Eisenhower. Dwight Eye-zen-how-er = Dwight Eisenhower
jimmy (a name, also a tool for prying things open) + cart (a small vehicle) + er = Carter. Jim-ee Cart-er = Jimmy Carter
west + on (out west, you're on the western side) + super (everything's going brilliantly, it's super) + mare (a female horse, a big impressive one) = Weston-super-Mare. West-on-super-mare.
hem (the edge of fabric) + el (a small one, a little) + hemp (a plant, associated with rope and fabric) + stead (an old word for a farmstead or homestead) = Hemel Hempstead. Hem-el-hemp-stead.
woke (awakened, roused from sleep) + ing (still going, ongoing) = Woking. Woke-ing.
tag (a label, or to tag someone) + lia (sounds like 'lear', or 'lia', a name) + tell (to tell someone off) + e (a sound at the end) = Tag-lia-tell-e = Tagliatelle
far (far away, a distance) + fall (to fall, or autumn) + e (a sound) = Far-fall-e = Farfalle. Farfalle means butterflies in Italian, which look like bow ties.
lingu (sounds like 'lingua', a tongue, or speech) + een (a long drawn-out sound) + e = Lingu-een-e = Linguine. Linguine means little tongues in Italian.
you (the word 'you', pointing at someone) + fray (sounds like 'fray', a fight or worn fabric) + tees (sounds like 'tees', the golf peg, or T-shirts) = You-fray-tees = Euphrates
zam (sounds like 'zam', a made-up sound, or 'Sam' with a Z) + be (the word 'be', to exist) + zee (sounds like 'zee', the American Z, or just 'ze') = Zam-be-zee = Zambezi
vol (sounds like 'vol', as in volume, quite loud) + ga (sounds like 'ga', a baby noise, or just a short grunt) = Vol-ga = Volga
im-press (to iron/press something, or to make an impression) + ion + ism = impressionism. Im-press-ion-ism. Press = iron your clothes.
bank (a financial institution, where you keep your money) + see (to look at something) = bank-see = Banksy. Bank + see said quickly.
ab (stomach muscle, an ab) + stract sounds like 'tract' = abstract, then express (a fast non-stop train) + ion + ism = abstract expressionism. Ab-stract + express-ion-ism.
kill (done away with) + him (a fella) + a (a) + jar (ceramic pot) + oh (there you go) = kill-him-a-jar-oh = Kilimanjaro

mont (sounds like 'mons' or 'more') + blanc (sounds like 'blank' or 'blonk') - 'mon' (French for my) + 'blanc' (blank/white) - sounds like 'mohn blank' = Mont Blanc. Phonetically: a matter that's blank = Mont Blanc
man (a bloke) + as (could've been) + loo (a toilet) = man-as-loo = Manaslu
heath + cliff = Heathcliff
cap + tin + hook = Captain Hook
long + john (loo) + silver = Long John Silver
jay (a blue aggressive garden bird) + son (a baby boy, a son) = jay-son = Jason.
maid (sounds like made, past tense of make) + Marian (a woman's name, Mary + an) = Maid Marian.
king (a monarch) + art (paintings, creative work) + her (a woman's possessive, the 'er' sound) = king-art-her = King Arthur.
den (animal sleeping room) + niece + Lewis... den + niece + is = Denise Lewis
Michael (archangel) + John + son = Michael Johnson
Derek + red + mond (round, like monde) = Derek Redmond
murky (foggy, mysterious swamp) + damp (everything's gone damp) = Murky Damp
spade (holding a spade) + ground (pointing at the ground) = Spade Ground
lamp (lamppost, lit up) + post (wrapped, sent like a parcel) = Lamp Post
boo (jumping out to scare someone) + duh (the sound you make when something is obvious) = Buddha
guru (a wise teacher) + nah (saying no firmly) + nack (sounds like 'knack', a skill) = Guru Nanak
eyed (watched, followed about) + al (the name Al) + fitter (in good shape, a fitness person) = Eid al-Fitr
zoo (a zoo) + him (him, shortened to 'm') = zoo-m = Zoom
slack (saggy and loose rope) = Slack
drop (letting go, watching fall) + box (cardboard container) = drop-box = Dropbox
stair + way + to + heaven = Stairway to Heaven
smells + like + teen + spirit = Smells Like Teen Spirit
don't + stop + me + now = Don't Stop Me Now
bur (prickly seed case that sticks to jumpers) + bun (soft bread roll) = Bourbon
custard (yellow wobbly pudding stuff) + cream (thick white dairy) = Custard Cream
Gar (Gary shortened) + ee + ball + dee = Garibaldi
pie (a pastry circle, or the Greek Pi symbol connection) + tha (sounds like 'the') + gore (a bit grim, gory) + us (us, you and me) = Pie-tha-gore-us = Pythagoras
log (a piece of wood, also a cabin) + a (the article) + rith (sounds like 'rhythm', a beat) + m (just the letter sound at the end) = Log-a-rith-m = Logarithm
hypo (sounds like 'hippo', a large animal, or 'hypa') + ten (the number 10) + use (to use something) = Hypo-ten-use = Hypotenuse
ac-cord-i-on. Accord (agreement, nodded along) + i + on (he's in, can't back out) = Accordion
didgeri-doo. Didgeri (sounds like 'Didgery', or Derek-y, a bloke called Derek) + doo (sounds like 'do', done a little job) = Didgeridoo. Derek + he + redo. Did + geri + do = Didgeridoo
there-min. There (in the cupboard, in there) + min (sounds like 'rummage' reduced, or 'min' as in minimum/small thing) = Theremin. 'In there' + 'min' = Theremin
black (dressed head to toe in the darkest colour) + widow (a woman who's lost her husband) = Black Widow.
gambit (a strategic opening move, calculated, could go either way) = Gambit.
dead (very quiet, still, not a ripple) + pool (a swimming area) = Deadpool.
steg (a bloke called Steg, or a male horse) + oh (a sound of mild realisation) + sore (in pain) + us (us, the people) = Steg-oh-sore-us = Stegosaurus
vel-oss (velocity without the 'ity', fast movement) + i (connecting sound) + rap (to wrap or strike) + tor (a rocky hill, or just the ending sound) = Vel-oss-i-rap-tor = Velociraptor
terror (terrified, a great fright) + oh (the sound) + dactyl (Greek for finger, like a digit doing backwards cycling) = Terror-oh-dactyl = Pterodactyl
arry (sounds like 'arri', informal version of Aristotle's start) + stotle (sounds like 'stott-ul', something being categorised vigorously) = Arri-stott-ul = Aristotle
plate (a plate of food, a flat round thing) + o (a surprised sound at the end) = Plate-o = Plato
kirk (a church, or the name Kirk, very common in Scandinavia) + e (a small sound) + gaard (sounds like 'guard', someone watching the door) = Kirk-e-gaard = Kierkegaard
drag (to pull someone forcibly) + ma (mum, mother) = Drag-ma = Drachma. Ancient Greek and former modern Greek currency.
crow (a large black bird) + na (a dismissive sound, like 'nah', short for no) = Crow-na = Krona. Swedish and other Scandinavian currencies.
flow (water moving through something) + ring (a piece of jewellery, a circular band) = Flow-ring = Florin. Old Dutch and British coin.
flame (a fire, proper burning thing) + en + co (short for company, like Ltd Co) = Flamenco
cha (informal word for tea, used in some parts) said twice = Cha Cha
ban (putting a stop to something officially) + gran (informal word for grandmother/nan) = Bhangra
su (sounds like 'sue', to take legal action) + shi (sounds like 'she', a woman) = Su-shi = Sushi
schnitz (sounds like 'snitz' or 'schnitts', a German-sounding whack) + el (the Spanish 'the', or a sound) = Schnitz-el = Schnitzel
brat (an unruly child, or the German word for fried) + wurst (German for sausage, sounds like 'worst') = Brat-wurst = Bratwurst
fen (a boggy marshy area) + you (the person standing in it) + greek (of Greek origin or nationality) = Fen-you-greek = Fenugreek
star (a celestial object, a bright thing in the sky) + an (the article) + ice (frozen water) = Star-an-ice = Star Anise
smoked (put through a smoker, like a kipper) + pap (soft food, or a gentle informal word) + reek (to smell strongly) + a (a small sound, a child's noise) = Smoked-pap-reek-a = Smoked Paprika
bite (had a nibble) + the + bull (cow in field) + it = Bite the Bullet
spill (tipped over, clumsy) + the + beans (legumes) = Spill the Beans
hit (lamped/whacked) + the + sack (potato sack) = Hit the Sack
mare (a horse) + cure (fix at the doctor's) + he = mer-cure-he = Mercury
or (a choice) + I (me) + on (carrying on, taking it on) = or-I-on = Orion
tie (to knot something) + tan (a beige/brownish colour) = tie-tan = Titan
east (gone east) + enders (ends of things) = EastEnders
coronation (crowning ceremony) + street (road) = Coronation Street
holly (name Holly) + oaks (oak trees, like in a tuck shop brand) = Hollyoaks
Wensley (a woman's name, unusual but there it is) + dale (a valley, like in the Dales) = Wensley-dale = Wensleydale. Say it out loud: Wensley + dale.
gorge on (to eat greedily, stuffing yourself) + Zola (Emile Zola, the French novelist) = gorge-on-Zola = Gorgonzola. Say it: gorge-on-Zola.
came in (arrived, turned up) + bear (a large furry animal) = came-in-bear = Camembert. Say it quickly: cam-em-bear.
prong (pointy bit) + horn (animal horn) = Pronghorn, the North American antelope
coo + do (like do-do), sounds like kudu, the striped African antelope
gnu sounds like new, or like you'd say it if you didn't know how
coq (sounds like 'cock', the male bird) + au (sounds like 'oh') + vin (sounds like 'van' or 'vin', wine in French) = Coq au Vin
chicken (the bird) + satay (sounds like 'sat-ay', someone sat on a stick, or 'sat-eh') = Chicken Satay
chicken (the bird) + shawarma (sounds like 'shaw-warm-a', someone called Shaw who's warm, or shawl-warmer) = Chicken Shawarma
ell (the letter L) + ton (massive weight) + john (toilet) = Elton John
Tom (cartoon cat) + Jones (Indiana Jones) = Tom Jones
cliff (rocky seaside drop) + rich (well-off) + ard (patch of yard) = Cliff Richard
woos (sounds like 'wuss', a bit soft) + ter (sounds like 'ta', thanks) + shire (a county in England, like Yorkshire) = Woos-ter-shire = Worcestershire
Holland (the country, flat, tulips) + daise (sounds like 'days', or 'daze', confused) = Holland-daise = Hollandaise
besh (sounds like 'besh', a French-ish sound) + a (the article) + mel (sounds like 'mel', a name, or honey in Latin) = Besh-a-mel = Bechamel
Peter (a saltpetre, a peter, rhyming with metre, a measure) + Kay (the letter K, as in a key) = Peter Kay. Pete + a key, say it out loud.
Bob (a man called Bob, bobbing about) + Mortimer (mort = death in French, timer = a clock counting down) = Bob Mortimer. Mort-timer, like a timer for the end of things.
Harry (to harry means to hassle or bother, or Harry as in hot and bothered) + Hill (a hill, an actual hill) = Harry Hill.
Pom (sounds like 'Pom', a nickname for a British person, or just the name Pom) + pay (sounds like 'pay', what you do at a cafe when the waiter's brought your thing) = Pom-pay = Pompeii
crack (sounds like 'crack', a loud bang, a fissure in rock) + a (just the letter, one of those) + toe (the smallest digit, end of the foot) + a (again, just the one) = Crack-a-toe-a = Krakatoa
Hiro (sounds like 'hero', someone who does something brave, or just the name Hiro) + shima (sounds like 'she-ma', or 'shima', meaning island in Japanese) = Hero-shima = Hiroshima
lay (lying flat on the ground) + miz-er-ah-bl (miserable, a state of total wretchedness) = lay-miz-er-abl = Les Miserables. Say it: lay + miserable.
ham (pig in a tin) + ill (not well, poorly) + ton (a heavy unit of weight, a lump of metal) = ham-ill-ton = Hamilton. Three sounds: ham + ill + ton.
shy (timid, embarrassed) + car (an automobile) + go (to drive off, to leave) = shy-car-go = Chicago. Say it out loud: shy-car-go = Chicago.
Jack + the + Ripper (the one doing the ripping) = Jack the Ripper
Billy (male goat name) + the + Kid (young goat = kid) = Billy the Kid
Bonnie (bonny = attractive) + and + Clyde (River Clyde area, Scotland) = Bonnie and Clyde

Gor (sounds like 'cor', an exclamation, bit surprised) + don (a powerful title, like a Don) + Ram (a male sheep, horns and everything) + say (to speak, to say a thing out loud) = Gor-don Ram-say = Gordon Ramsay

Hes (sounds like 'he's', short for he is) + ton (a unit of weight, very heavy) + Bloom (when a flower opens up) + en (a small sound, like 'un') + thal (sounds like 'thal', a bit like 'thaw' or 'Neanderthal') = Hes-ton Bloom-en-thal = Heston Blumenthal

Nye (sounds like 'nye', as in the letter N said out loud, or just a no-ish sound) + jell + a (sounds like 'jella', like jelly gone wobbly) + Law (the police, rules, order) + son (a male child, your boy) = Nye-jell-a Law-son = Nigella Lawson
peek (to look quickly, hide and seek style) + a + choo (sounds like 'chu', a small squeak or sneeze) = Pikachu. Peek-a-choo.
mew (a soft cry, like a cat) + two (there's two of them, an original and a clone) = Mewtwo. Mew-two.
ala (sounds like a magician's setup, 'ala-') + ka + zam (the big magic reveal sound) = Alakazam. Al-a-ka-zam.
foss (sounds like 'foss', as in fossil, or a ditch) + for (to move something on behalf of someone) + us (me and you, us lot) = Foss-for-us = Phosphorus
mag (sounds like 'Mag', short for Maggie) + knee (the joint, goes wrong) + see (to look at something) + um (that noise you make when you're not sure) = Mag-nee-see-um = Magnesium
floo (sounds like 'flew', past tense of fly, or 'flue', a drain pipe) + reen (sounds like 'reen', a drain in West Country slang, or just 'reen') = Floo-reen = Fluorine
say-ga (Sega) + mega (massive, powerful) + drive (a lap, driving round) = Sega Mega Drive
game (game, entertainment, up for it) + boy (a lad, Boy as a name) = Game Boy
super (excellent, better than rest) + nin-ten-do (ten things to do) = Super Nintendo
snow (winter precipitation) + white (the colour) = Snow White
her (she/her) + cools (makes things cool) + ease (with no difficulty) = Hercules
monsters (terrifying creatures) + ink (the writing fluid) = Monsters Inc
The (the) + Anchor (heavy metal thing dropped from a boat to keep it in place, an anchor) = The Anchor
The (the) + Nags (old worn-out horses, nags) + Head (the head, what's on the sign) = The Nags Head
The (the) + Chequers (a checked black and white pattern, chequers, very common pub name) = The Chequers
rigg (sounds like 'rig', or the name Rigger) + oh (a sound of mild resignation) + let (to allow something) + oh (another mild sound at the end) = Rig-oh-let-oh = Rigoletto
nab (to grab or steal something, very casual theft) + uck (a sound of mild disgust or surprise) + oh (a trailing sound as the consequences unfold) = Nab-uck-oh = Nabucco

loo (a toilet, or the name Lu, someone called Lou going somewhere urgent) + chee (a sharp little sound, like teeth) + ah (a sigh of relief or despair) + dee (the letter D as a word, meaning of, sort of) + lammer (sounds like 'lam-uh', like a lamb having a go) + moor (a soggy bit of land in the north of England or Scotland, very bleak) = Loo-chee-ah dee Lam-uh-moor = Lucia di Lammermoor

ge (sounds like 'geh', a German noise of mild concern) + wurz (sounds like 'wurts', worries or worsted) + tram (sounds like 'tram', a vehicle, or traminer, a trainer on a tram) + iner (sounds like 'iner', a trainer suffix) = Ge-wurz-tram-iner = Gewurztraminer

temp (sounds like 'temp', temporary, not permanent, just passing through) + ran (sounds like 'ran', he ran and fell) + illo (sounds like 'illo', a Spanish diminutive, little) = Temp-ran-illo = Tempranillo
nebb (sounds like 'neb', a beak or a nosy snout) + iolo (sounds like 'ee-oh-lo', a little noise at the end, or 'nebbia' meaning fog in Italian) = Nebb-iolo = Nebbiolo
you (addressing us directly) + ca + lip (the thing on your face) + tus (sounds like 'tuss', a fuss) = you-ca-lip-tus = Eucalyptus
bay (a bay window) + oh (an exclamation) + bab (a term of endearment, like calling someone bab) = bay-oh-bab = Baobab
weeping (having a good cry) + will (they're going to do it) + ow (an exclamation of resignation, sounds like 'oh') = weeping-will-ow = Weeping Willow
mal (sounds like 'Mal' or 'Molly' shortened) + a (just the letter a, a pause) + yalam (sounds like 'yalarm', as in 'her alarm') = Mal-a-yalam = Malayalam
ketch (a type of two-masted sailing boat) + wah (the sound a baby makes when it's not happy) = Ketch-wah = Quechua
cat (a furry animal, sits on things) + a (just a, the article) + lan (sounds like 'Lan', a Welsh-ish short name, or 'land' with the d gone quiet) = Cat-a-lan = Catalan
wins + ton = Winston, church + hill = Churchill. Wins-ton-Church-hill.
rich + ard = Richard, the + lion + heart = the Lionheart. Rich-ard-the-lion-heart.
hen + ree = Henry, VIII = eight. Hen-ree-eight.
atom (the smallest unit of an element, which bonds with other atoms) = Atom
spectrum (the range of colours visible when light is dispersed) = Spectrum
mix (combining two things) + ture = Mixture
sigh (long breath) + co (company) = Psycho
ghost (departed this world) + bus (vehicle) + ters (sounds like 'ters') = Ghostbusters
far (long way to travel) + go (leave) = Fargo
Singapore (the city-state at the tip of the Malay peninsula) + sling (a bandage support for an injured arm) = Singapore Sling. Say it: Singapore-Sling.
te-kill-a (to kill her, sounds like tequila said fast: te-kill-a) + sunrise (the sun coming up over the horizon in the morning) = Tequila Sunrise. Say it out loud: te-kill-a sun-rise.
neg (to neg someone, give a backhanded compliment or put-down) + row (to argue, or to row a boat) + knee (the joint in the middle of your leg) = neg-row-nee = Negroni.
ty (sounds like 'tie', to tie something up) + phoon (sounds like 'foon', or 'phone' said quickly without the e) = Ty-phoon = Typhoon
tsu (sounds like 'soo', as in Sue, a name) + nami (sounds like 'nah-me', or 'nammy', like a nan figure) = Soo-nami = Tsunami
mon (sounds like 'mon', as in Monday, or 'mon' meaning my in some languages) + soon (sounds like 'soon', not long now) = Mon-soon = Monsoon
Steve + in + gear + rd = Steven Gerrard. Steve + in = Steven, gear + rd = Gerrard
Gary + line + ker = Gary Lineker. Line = the mark, -ker = suffix making it a person who lines
Day + vid + beck + ham = David Beckham. Beck = stream, ham = place with pigs/meat
cue (billiards stick) + ba sounds like cyoo-ba = Cuba
too (excessive) + knee (leg joint) + sha sounds like too-nee-sha = Tunisia
fuerte (strong in Spanish, sounds like fwer-tay) + venture (adventure) + a = Fuerteventura
mad (lost the plot, gone mad) + a (the article) + gas (fuel, what the car runs on) + car (the vehicle) = Mad-a-gas-car = Madagascar
bore (to bore someone, to be dull) + knee (part of the leg) + oh (a sound of mild realisation) = Bore-knee-oh = Borneo
sue (to take legal action, or the name Sue) + ma (mum, mother) + tra (sounds like 'tram' without the m, or 'tra' as in tra-la-la) = Sue-ma-tra = Sumatra
fish (a fish, an aquatic creature) + ing (doing a hobby, present participle) = Fish-ing = Phishing
ran (sprinted, past tense of run) + som (sounds like 'sum', a piece of arithmetic) + ware (something you wear, or software) = Ran-som-ware = Ransomware
