tea (the hot brown drink) + spoon (a little shovel, a spoon) = Teaspoon
table (flat piece of furniture in the dining room) + cloth (big bit of fabric covering it) = Tablecloth
foot (the bottom of someone's leg) + print (pressed onto paper, left a mark) = Footprint
hand (the bit at the end of your arm) + bag (turned into a carrier, handles, take to the shops) = Handbag
note (a single musical note) + book (written itself a novel, a thick book) = Notebook
wall (the great flat thing in the living room) + paper (reading the news, a broadsheet paper) = Wallpaper
tooth (the thing in your mouth you chew with) + paste (glued itself, stuck fast, paste is glue) = Toothpaste
wind (the breeze) + screen (a telly, a flat screen) = Windscreen
dust (little bits of muck from under the sofa) + bin (thrown themselves away, into the bin) = Dustbin
cob (a corn cob, sounds like the front of cobweb) + web (the spider's sticky net) = Cobweb
ear (the side of your head, you hear with it) + drum (a little drum strapped there) = Eardrum
skate (rolling on wheels, gliding) + board (a flat board strapped to your feet) = Skateboard
clock (the clock, tick tock) + work (given a job, routine, going round) = Clockwork
suit (packed all their stuff, like a business suit in a bag) + case (the box, the case you travel with) = Suitcase
cross (going across, and the pattern of the grid which is like a cross) + word (you fill in words) = Crossword
life (guarding people's lives at the pool) + guard (stationed there watching, a guard) = Lifeguard
el (like 'ell', an exclamation of surprise) + bow (bending, like a bow shape at the joint) = Elbow
skate (surfed/glided down the hill) + board (a flat piece of wood) = Skateboard
black (gone completely dark, midnight black) + bird (every feather, sitting in a tree) = Blackbird
sun (the big yellow thing in the sky) + flower (given off blooms, petals everywhere) = Sunflower
thunder (the rumbling, rattling the ornaments) + storm (moved indoors, gone wild) = Thunderstorm
water (flat liquid) + fall (tripped over, going down fast) = Waterfall
green (painted entirely green) + house (a little building for plants) = Greenhouse
foot (the shoe, the bottom of the leg) + print (left a mark, pressed down on paper or ground) = Footprint
note (a single musical tone written down) + book (filled up a whole volume) = Notebook
corn (yellow grain) + flakes (gone crumbly, little bits everywhere) = Cornflakes
wall (all round the room, covering the walls) + paper (printed sheets, newspaper type material) = Wallpaper
tooth (the thing in your gob for biting) + paste (stuck to everything, sticky paste) = Toothpaste
wind (the moving air outside) + screen (a glass panel, a screen) = Windscreen
cob (a corn cob, big round yellow thing with kernels) + web (spun a sticky net, like a spider's web) = Cobweb
ear (the side of your head, you hear with it) + drum (a little drum, strapped on) = Eardrum
dust (grey powder and fluff from the carpet) + bin (the big container outside) = Dustbin
hand (the thing at the end of your arm) + bag (sewn onto a carrier, handles, you take it shopping) = Handbag
tea (the brew, your cuppa) + spoon (a little scooping device in the saucer) = Teaspoon
table (the flat thing in the middle of the dining room) + cloth (dressed in a big white sheet, a cloth) = Tablecloth
cross (the squares go across, and the grid shape crosses over) + word (the words you fill in) = Crossword
life (keeping everyone safe, guarding their lives) + guard (standing watch, a guard on a tall chair) = Lifeguard
black (shed all colour, jet black) + bird (still flying about, a bird) = Blackbird
butter (the margarine, the spread) + fly (sprouted wings, taken off, gone out the window) = Butterfly
blue (bright sky blue) + bell (it's ringing, the bell shape of the flower) = Bluebell
suit (everything they own, their whole suit of belongings) + case (a single box, clicked shut) = Suitcase
skate (rolling on wheels, gliding about) + board (a flat piece of wood with wheels bolted on) = Skateboard
clock (the mechanism inside the clock, the cogs and wheels) + work (it's got a shift to do, repetitive work) = Clockwork
door (the big entrance with the letterbox) + step (someone stood right on the step) = Doorstep
fire (the flames) + works (clocked in, got a badge, doing the job) = Fireworks
el (sounds like 'ell', at the joint where the arm bends) + bow (a bow and arrow, or the bowing shape of the bent arm) = Elbow
cob (round-shaped thing, like a corn cob) + web (sticky string, the spider's net) = Cobweb
wall (the walls in here) + paper (printed material, flowers and stripes, wallpaper) = Wallpaper
tooth (the thing in your mouth you chew with) + paste (thick white paste squeezed onto it) = Toothpaste
butter (the dairy spread, needed for toast) + fly (taken flight, out the window) = Butterfly
pry (to force open) + mark (a visible mark left behind) = Primark. Say it: pry-mark.
top (the best, the highest) + shop (a place to buy things) = Topshop. Say it: top-shop.
new (different, changed) + look (your appearance) = New Look. Say it: new-look.
river (a flowing body of water) + island (a piece of land surrounded by water) = River Island. Say it: river-island.
necks (what giraffes have loads of) sounds like Next. Say it: necks = Next.
marks (scribbles, marks on a wall) + and + Spencer (a bloke's name) = Marks and Spencer. Say it: marks-and-Spencer.
burr (a prickly seed case) + bury (to put underground) = Burberry. Say it: burr-bury = Burberry.
Paul (a man's name) + Smith (a blacksmith, someone who works metal) = Paul Smith. Say it: Paul-Smith.
goo (sticky, messy substance) + chee (sounds like the end of 'cheese') = Gucci. Say it: goo-chee = Gucci.
Viv + ee + anne (Vivienne) + west (direction, far end) + wood (timber, forest) = Vivienne Westwood. Say it: Viv-ee-anne West-wood.
verse (a poem, a verse of writing) + ace (brilliant, a champion) = Versace. Say it: verse-ace-y = Versace.
pray (to say prayers) + da (dad, informal) = Prada. Say it: pray-da = Prada.
Alex + and + her (Alexander) + Mac (a computer) + queen (royalty, top dog) = Alexander McQueen. Say it: Alex-and-her Mac-queen.
Stella (a name) + Mac (a computer, or mac coat) + cart (a wheeled vehicle) + knee (the joint in your leg) = Stella McCartney. Say it: Stella Mac-cart-knee.
jimmy (to jimmy open, to pry/force a lock) + choo (sounds like chew, to chew something) = Jimmy Choo. Say it: jimmy-choo.
Christian (a name) + Lou (a name, short for Louise) + boo (to scare someone) + tin (a metal tin) = Christian Louboutin. Say it: Christian-Lou-boo-tin.
shan't (won't do it, informal refusal) sounds like 'sha' + Nell (a name) = Chanel. Say it: sha-nell = Chanel.
Louie (a name, Louis) + wee (a small amount, or to wee) + ton (a unit of weight, very heavy) = Louis Vuitton. Say it: Louie-wee-ton = Louis Vuitton.
balance (to keep steady, not fall over) + ee + ah + ga = Balenciaga. Say it: bal-en-see-ah-ga = Balenciaga.
car (a vehicle) + heart (the organ, or meaning passion) = Carhartt. Say it: car-heart = Carhartt.
Harvey (a name) + Nichols (a surname, sounds like Nicholas) = Harvey Nichols. Say it: Harvey-Nichols.
self (oneself, own) + ridges (raised strips, or Ridges as in surname) = Selfridges. Say it: self-ridges = Selfridges.
John (a name) + Lewis (a surname, sounds like 'leaves' + 's' or 'lew-is') = John Lewis. Say it: John-Lewis.
off (slightly wrong, gone bad) + white (the colour) = Off-White. Say it: off-white.
supreme (the greatest, the best, above all others) = Supreme. The brand name is its own phonetic clue, sounds like 'sue-preem'.
her (she, belonging to her) + mess (a state of chaos and disorder) = Hermès. Say it: her-mess = Hermès.
pry (to force open, to lever) + Mark (a man's name) = Primark. Say it: pry-mark = Primark.
Vivienne (Viv + ee + anne) + West (direction, heading west) + wood (a forest, woods) = Vivienne Westwood.
goo (slime, sticky mess) + chee (like the end of 'cheesy', a chewy sound) = Gucci. Say it: goo-chee = Gucci.
necks (the part of the body, plural, giraffes have long necks) sounds like Next. Say it: necks = Next.
river (flowing water, a river) + island (surrounded by water, land mass) = River Island.
burr (a prickly seed case that sticks to clothing) + bury (to put in the ground, inter) = Burberry. Say it: burr-bury = Burberry.
car (a vehicle) + heart (devotion, love for something) = Carhartt. Say it: car-heart = Carhartt.
jimmy (to jimmy open a door, to lever/force) + choo (sounds like chew, to masticate) = Jimmy Choo.
pray (to pray, to be in prayer) + da (dad, informal term for father) = Prada. Say it: pray-da = Prada.
Alex + and + her (= Alexander) + Mac (laptop brand, informal) + queen (royalty, on the throne) = Alexander McQueen.
Paul (a man's first name) + Smith (a blacksmith, a metal worker at a forge) = Paul Smith.
verse (a unit of poetry, a stanza) + ace (excellent, brilliant, top quality) = Versace. Say it: verse-ace-ee = Versace.
her (belonging to her, feminine pronoun possessive) + mess (disorder, chaos, a terrible state) = Hermès. Say it: her-mess = Hermès.
Christian (a name) + Lou (a name) + boo (to shout boo, to frighten) + tin (a metal container) = Christian Louboutin. Say it: Christian-Lou-boo-tin.
Louie (Louis, a name) + wee (tiny, or a small accident) + ton (a unit of weight, very heavy) = Louis Vuitton. Say it: Louie-wee-ton = Louis Vuitton.
shan't (I shan't, I will not, a refusal) sounds like 'sha' + Nell (a woman's name) = Chanel. Say it: sha-nell = Chanel.
self (oneself, your own) + ridges (Ridges, like a surname, Self + ridges = Selfridges). Say it: self-ridges = Selfridges.
balance (to stay steady, equilibrium) + ee + ah + ga = Balenciaga. Say it: bal-en-see-ah-ga.
off (not quite right, slightly gone) + white (the colour) = Off-White. Say it: off-white.
marks (the traces or blemishes left behind) + and + Spencer (a person's surname) = Marks and Spencer.
supreme (the highest authority, the most powerful, above all others) = Supreme. The word itself is the phonetic clue.
top (highest, the best, at the pinnacle) + shop (a retail establishment) = Topshop.
John (a man's name) + Lewis (a surname, sounds like 'lew-iss') = John Lewis. Say it: John-Lewis.
Harvey (a name) + Nichols (a surname, sounds like Nicholas) = Harvey Nichols.
new (changed, different, fresh) + look (appearance, aesthetic) = New Look.
Stella (a name) + Mac (informal for a mackintosh, or Mac computer) + cart (a trolley, shopping cart) + knee (the leg joint) = Stella McCartney.
pry (to pry open, to use a lever to force something) + mark (a visible impression left behind) = Primark. Say it: pry-mark.
car (a vehicle) + heart (the cardiac organ, or emotion) = Carhartt. Say it: car-heart = Carhartt. Note: alternate set uses this differently, car + heart both work.
verse (written poetry, a stanza of verse) + ace (brilliant, top quality, the best) = Versace. Say it: verse-ace-ee.
goo (sticky slime-like substance) + chee (sounds like the end of 'cheese', chewy sound) = Gucci. Say it: goo-chee.
her (she, feminine possessive pronoun) + mess (a state of disorder and chaos) = Hermès. Say it: her-mess = Hermès.
burr (the prickly seed case from plants, sticks to fabric) + bury (to put underground, to inter) = Burberry. Say it: burr-bury = Burberry.
shan't (refusal, won't do it, 'I shan't') sounds like sha + Nell (a name) = Chanel. Alternate: the channel without the 'ch', sha-nell = Chanel.
Louie (Louis, a name pronounced Louie) + wee (tiny, a small amount) + ton (a unit of weight, very heavy) = Louis Vuitton. Say it: Louie-wee-ton = Louis Vuitton.
The + Sun = The Sun. 'The' sounds like 'the', 'Sun' sounds like 'sun' (the star). Silly scene: just pointing at the actual sun.
The + Mirror = The Mirror. Mirror sounds like a reflective surface. Scene: a mirror image in water.
Heat + Mag-a-zine. Heat sounds like warmth, mag sounds like 'mag' (short shout). Scene: warm shout across a room.
The + Guard + Ian = The Guardian. 'The guard Ian' said fast = The Guardian. Scene: someone called Ian being guarded.
I + T + V = ITV. 'I' (me) + 'T' (tea) + 'V' (vee/see). Actually, I-T-V said as letters. Scene: 'I' see a 'TV'. I-TV.
T-V + Times = TV Times. Tee-Vee + Times. Scene: a printed schedule of television programmes, a telly timetable.
B-B-C + One = BBC One. Bee-Bee-See + Won/One. Scene: the original BBC channel, the first one they ever did.
Capital + FM = Capital FM. 'Capital' (main city) + FM (radio frequency). Scene: a capital city phoning in on a crystal clear FM signal.
The + Express = The Express. 'Express' means to say something strongly. Scene: strongly expressing views in a newspaper.
Channel + Four = Channel 4. 'Chan-nel' sounds like a waterway/passage, 'four' is the number 4. Scene: someone counting a channel up to four.
Daily + Mail = Daily Mail. 'Daily' (every day) + 'Mail' (post). Scene: daily postal delivery, simple as that.
OK + Magazine = OK Magazine. 'OK' is an expression of agreement. Scene: responding that things are fine, 'OK', then grabbing a magazine.
The + Independent = The Independent. 'Independent' means standing alone, not relying on others. Scene: a newspaper going it alone, independent from influence.
Radio + Times = Radio Times. 'Radio' (wireless broadcasting) + 'Times' (schedule/timetable). Scene: a published guide to radio programme schedules.
Gold = Gold (TV channel). 'Gold' sounds like gold, precious, classic, old. Scene: a TV channel full of classic programmes described as gold.
The + Times = The Times. 'Times' sounds like occasions/schedule. Scene: a newspaper that reports the times of things, events, goings on.
Magic + FM = Magic FM. 'Magic' (like a conjuring trick) + FM (radio). Scene: a radio station themed around something magical.
Channel + Five = Channel 5. Same as Channel 4 but with five. Scene: counting all the way up to five on a waterway.
Heart + FM = Heart FM. 'Heart' (the organ) + FM (radio frequency). Scene: a healthy beating heart on FM radio.
The + Telegraph = The Telegraph. 'Telegraph' (old messaging system using wires). Scene: a message sent by telegraph, old fashioned communication in print form.
Dave = Dave (TV channel). 'Dave' is a very ordinary bloke's name. Scene: a channel for regular fellas called Dave.
Sky + One = Sky One. 'Sky' (what's up above) + 'One' (the first). Scene: looking up at the sky and it being the number one view.
Take + a + Break = Take a Break. 'Take a break' is a common phrase meaning to have a rest. Scene: someone pausing for a small rest.
Closer = Closer (magazine). 'Closer' means nearer. Scene: someone getting unexpectedly close to another person.
BBC + Radio + Two = BBC Radio 2. Bee-Bee-See + Radio (wireless) + Two (second one). Scene: the BBC making a second radio station.
Hello + Magazine = Hello Magazine. 'Hello' (a greeting) + 'Magazine' (glossy booklet). Scene: greeting a celebrity and publishing it in a magazine.
News + of + the + World = News of the World. 'News' + 'of the World' (globally significant). Scene: worldwide news breaking on a Sunday paper.
Alibi = Alibi (TV channel). 'Alibi' is a word for a secret defence, where you were when the crime happened. Scene: a channel all about secrets and crime.
The + Express = The Express. 'Express' (direct, fast, clear). Scene: a very direct letter delivered quickly and without ambiguity.
Sunday + Sport = Sunday Sport. 'Sunday' (day of the week) + 'Sport' (athletic activities). Scene: a sports-only Sunday newspaper.
The + Sun = The Sun. 'Sun' (the star, the warmth, the light). Scene: a bright shining newspaper named after the actual sun in the sky.
BBC + One = BBC One. Bee-Bee-See + One. Scene: the main BBC channel, the primary one, the number one.
The + Independent = The Independent. 'Independent' (self-reliant). Scene: a newspaper that stands alone without support.
The + Mirror = The Mirror. 'Mirror' (reflective surface). Scene: a daily newspaper that reflects current events back at you.
Capital + FM = Capital FM. 'Capital' (principal city, like London) + FM (radio). Scene: the capital city calling a radio station with a clear FM signal.
The + Guard + Ian = The Guardian. 'The guard Ian' said quickly = The Guardian. Scene: a bodyguard watching over a fella called Ian.
Heart + FM = Heart FM. 'Heart' (the cardiac organ) + FM (radio frequency). Scene: the heart literally broadcasting on FM radio.
Daily + Mail = Daily Mail. 'Daily' (each day) + 'Mail' (letters and post). Scene: the morning post arriving every single day.
Radio + Times = Radio Times. 'Radio' (broadcasting) + 'Times' (scheduled times). Scene: a printed timetable of all radio programmes.
TV + Times = TV Times. Tee-Vee + Times (schedule). Scene: a printed television schedule covering all the programmes.
I + TV = ITV. 'I' (me) + 'TV' (television). Scene: a person finding a television and them both looking at each other.
Alibi = Alibi (TV channel). 'Al-i-bi' sounds like having a confirmed excuse. Scene: someone with a rock-solid alibi for not being at the scene.
The + Times = The Times. 'Times' (moments in time, a schedule of events). Scene: a newspaper recording exactly when things happened.
Magic + FM = Magic FM. 'Magic' (conjuring, tricks) + FM (radio). Scene: a magician performing on a radio station.
Closer = Closer (magazine). 'Closer' (moving nearer). Scene: someone leaning in to get a closer look at something.
Sky + One = Sky One. 'Sky' (the atmosphere above) + 'One' (first, primary). Scene: looking up and the sky being the number one view.
The + Telegraph = The Telegraph. 'Telegraph' (wired message system from the old days). Scene: a news message arriving by telegraph wire and going straight to print.
BBC + Radio + Two = BBC Radio 2. Bee-Bee-See + Radio + Two. Scene: the BBC expanding to a second radio channel.
Hello + Magazine = Hello Magazine. 'Hello' (warm greeting) + 'Magazine' (glossy publication). Scene: a warm celebrity greeting ending up in a magazine.
OK + Magazine = OK Magazine. 'OK' (everything's fine) + Magazine. Scene: someone confirming all is well, then it appearing in a magazine.
Dave = Dave (TV channel). 'Dave' is an archetypal ordinary bloke's name. Scene: a TV channel so ordinary, it's named after the most ordinary name going.
Take + a + Break = Take a Break. 'Take a break' said naturally. Scene: someone being told to take a short rest.
News + of + the + World = News of the World. Scene: a story so big, the whole world heard about it. Published on Sundays.
Gold = Gold (TV channel). 'Gold' (precious, classic, old). Scene: a channel described as being full of golden, classic programmes.
Sunday + Sport = Sunday Sport. 'Sunday' (the day) + 'Sport' (athletics, games). Scene: a Sunday-only sports newspaper.
Channel + Four = Channel 4. 'Channel' (a passage, like a TV channel) + 'Four' (the number). Scene: counting to four and stopping there.
The + Mirror = The Mirror. 'Mirror' (reflecting something back). Scene: speaking directly to someone, mirroring the truth back at them.
Channel + Five = Channel 5. 'Channel' (TV passage) + 'Five' (number 5). Scene: counting up to five, going one further than last time.
The + Telegraph = The Telegraph. 'Telegraph' (wire-based messaging). Scene: old telegraph messages compiled and printed as a daily newspaper.
The + Guard + Ian = The Guardian. 'The guard Ian' said fast = The Guardian. Scene: a minder keeping watch over a bloke called Ian.
Queen = Kween, Victoria = Vic-TOR-ia. Queen sounds like 'keen' with a qu. Victoria sounds like 'victor-ia'. The scene is a queen with a victory, a win. Queen Victoria. Near enough.
King Henry VIII. King = King. Henry = Hen-ree. VIII = the eighth. Henry sounds like 'hen' + 'ree'. So a king with a hen called Ree who's done this eight times. Bit weird but say it out loud.
Anne Boleyn. Anne = Ann. Boleyn = Bowl-in. Ann + bowling = Anne Boleyn. She's literally ann-bowl-in. Say it out loud.

Prince William. Prince = Prints (press/print). William = Will-yam. Prince sounds like 'prints'. Prints + Will + yam. Bit of a stretch. Better: Prince = Prin-ce, William = Will-ee-um. A printing press... no. Prince William. Prince sounds like 'prance' without the a. Actually: Prin-ce Will-iam = a fella called Will who is leaning like a post, a PRINCE of posts. Say it out loud, it works.

Prince Harry. Prince = Prints/Prance. Harry = Hairy/Harr-ee. Prince Harry. Prin-ce Harr-ee. A prince who's hairy. Or: prints + harry. He's printed and he's harrying someone. Say it out loud: Prince Harry. Near enough.
Wallis Simpson. Wallis = Wall-is. Simpson = Simp-son. Wall-is Simp-son. There's a wall, it is simple, it's a son. Wall-is-Simp-son. Say it out loud: Wallis Simpson. Works.
King George. King = King. George = Jor-j. King George, sounds like 'kin-George'. A king called George, who sounds like he's gorging on something. King George. Easy one this.
Prince Albert. Prince = Prints. Albert = Al-bert. Prints-Al-bert. He's printing things and his name's Albert. A print-Albert. Prince Albert. Say it out loud.
Queen Camilla. Queen = Kween. Camilla = Cam-ill-a. Cam (camera) + ill + a. She's got a camera and she's feeling a bit poorly. Cam-ill-a. Queen Camilla. Near enough.
Queen Elizabeth. Queen = Kween. Elizabeth = Ee-liz-a-beth. Ee-liz, as in electric lizard, a-beth. A queen who's an electric lizard called Beth. Queen Elizabeth. Say it out loud.
Prince Philip. Prince = Prints. Philip = Fill-up. Prints-Fill-up. He's printing something and filling it up. A print-fill-up. Prince Philip. Near enough.
King Charles. King = King. Charles = Char-lz. King Charles, sounds like 'kin-charlz'. A king called Charles who's charring things. Bit weird but it works.
Princess Anne. Princess = Prin-cess. Anne = Ann. Prin-cess-Ann. A princess called Ann who's been running around. Say it out loud: Princess Anne. Easy one this.

Princess Beatrice. Princess = Prin-cess. Beatrice = Bee-a-triss. Bee (insect) + a + triss (trice, as in a short time). A princess who's a bee doing something in a trice. Princess Beatrice. Say it out loud.

Princess Eugenie. Princess = Prin-cess. Eugenie = You-jean-ee. You + jean + ee. It's yours, it's a pair of jeans, and it's got an ee sound. A princess whose name sounds like 'you-jeans'. Princess Eugenie. Near enough.
Prince Andrew. Prince = Prints. Andrew = An-drew. Prints-An-drew. He's printing and drawing. A print-an-drew. Prince Andrew. Say it out loud.
Prince Edward. Prince = Prints. Edward = Ed-word. Ed + word. Someone called Ed's got a word. Or: Ed-ward, like a ward in a hospital run by Ed. Prince Edward. Near enough.

Zara Tindall. Zara = Zar-a. Tindall = Tin-dall. Tin + doll. She's got a tin and a doll. Or Tindall sounds like 'tindle', tinder/kindle. Zara = the clothes shop. A Zara shop with a tinder problem. Zara Tindall. Say it out loud.
Meghan Markle. Meghan = Meg-an. Markle = Mar-kul. Meg-an-Mar-kul. Megan at the market, but the market's a bit awkward about it. Meghan Markle. Say it out loud.
Peter Phillips. Peter = Pee-ter. Phillips = Fill-ips. Fill-ips. Peter who fills things up. Peter Fill-ups. Peter Phillips. Near enough.
Lady Louise. Lady = Lay-dee. Louise = Lou-eeze. Lay-dee-Lou-eeze. A lady who's easing something out. Lady Louise. Say it out loud.
Catherine of Aragon. Catherine = Kath-rin. Of = ov. Aragon = Arr-a-gon. Kath-rin-of-Arr-a-gon. A Catherine who's gone off somewhere. Catherine of Aragon. Say it out loud.
King Edward VIII. King = King. Edward = Ed-word. VIII = the-eighth. Ed-word-the-eighth. A king called Ed who has a word and does it eighth. King Edward VIII. Near enough.
Princess Catherine. Princess = Prin-cess. Catherine = Kath-rin. Prin-cess-Kath-rin. A princess called Kath who's running things. Princess Catherine. Say it out loud.
Queen Victoria. Queen = Kween. Victoria = Vik-tor-ee-a. Victor + ia. She's the queen who victoried. Queen Victoria. Easy one this.
Prince Albert. Prince = Prints. Albert = Al-bert. Al + bert. Someone called Al has a bert, a Bert is a name. Al-bert. Prints-Al-bert. Say it out loud: Prince Albert. Near enough.
King Henry VIII. King + Henry + the Eighth. Hen-ree the Eighth. A king who has a hen called Ree who's done this eighth time. King Henry VIII. Say it out loud.
Prince William. Prince = Prints. William = Will-yam. Prints + Will-yam. A printing press run by a bloke called William. Prince William. Say it out loud.
Anne Boleyn. Anne = Ann. Boleyn = Bowl-in. Ann + bowling. She's Ann and she's bowl-in. Anne Boleyn. Say it out loud.
Wallis Simpson. Wallis = Wall-is. Simpson = Simp-son. Wall + is + simple + son. There's a wall, it is simple, and there's a son. Wallis Simpson. Near enough.
Queen Elizabeth. Queen = Kween. Elizabeth = Ee-liz-a-beth. Electric + Beth. She's electric and she's a Beth. Queen Elizabeth. Say it out loud.
Princess Anne. Princess = Prin-cess. Anne = Ann. Prin-cess Ann. A princess called Ann who's been running about. Princess Anne. Easy one this.
Princess Eugenie. Eugenie = You-jean-ee. You + jean + ee. It's yours, it's jeans, and it ends in ee. Princess Eugenie. Near enough.
King George. King = King. George = Jaw-j. Jaw + j. He's a king with a working jaw. Georged his dinner. King George. Say it out loud.
Prince Harry. Prince = Prints. Harry = Hairy. Prin-ts + Hairy. A printer who's hairy. Prince Harry. Near enough.
Meghan Markle. Meghan = Meg-an. Markle = Mar-kul. Megan at the market, sort of. Meg-an-Mar-kul. Meghan Markle. Say it out loud.
Prince Philip. Prince = Prints. Philip = Fill-up. Prints + Fill-up. He's printing and filling. Prince Philip. Say it out loud.
Prince Andrew. Prince = Prints. Andrew = An-drew. An + drew. He's done a drawing. Prince Andrew. Near enough.
Zara Tindall. Zara = Zar-a. Tindall = Tin-doll. Tin + doll. A Zara with a tin doll. Zara Tindall. Say it out loud.
Princess Catherine. Princess = Prin-cess. Catherine = Kath-rin. Prin-cess + Kath-rin. A princess called Kath. Princess Catherine. Easy one this.
Queen Camilla. Queen = Kween. Camilla = Cam-ill-a. Camera + ill + a. She's got a cam and she's a bit ill. Queen Camilla. Say it out loud.
King Charles. King = King. Charles = Char-lz. Char + lz. He's charring. King Charles. Near enough.
Lady Louise. Lady = Lay-dee. Louise = Lou-eeze. Lay-dee + Lou-eeze. A lady who's easing in. Lady Louise. Say it out loud.
Peter Phillips. Peter = Pee-ter. Phillips = Fill-ups. Fill + ups. He fills things up. Peter Phillips. Near enough.
Catherine of Aragon. Catherine = Kath-rin. Of = of. Aragon = Arr-a-gon. A-gone. She's gone. Catherine of Aragon. Say it out loud.
Princess Beatrice. Princess = Prin-cess. Beatrice = Bee-a-triss. Bee + a + trice. A bee who's done it in a trice. Princess Beatrice. Say it out loud.
King Edward VIII. King = King. Edward = Ed-word. VIII = the-eighth. King Ed with a word, eight times over. King Edward VIII. Near enough.
Anne Boleyn. Anne = Ann. Boleyn = Bowl-in. Ann + bowling. She's always bowl-in. Anne Boleyn. Say it out loud.
Queen Victoria. Queen = Kween. Victoria = Vik-tor-ee-a. Victor + ia. A victorious queen. Queen Victoria. Easy one this.
Wallis Simpson. Wallis = Wall-is. Simpson = Simp-son. A wall that is simple and belongs to a son. Wallis Simpson. Say it out loud.
Prince Harry. Prince = Prints. Harry = Hairy. Prints + Hairy. A printer who's extremely hairy. Prince Harry. Near enough.
King Henry VIII. King = King. Henry = Hen-ree. VIII = the-eighth. A king called Henry who's done it eighth. King Henry VIII. Say it out loud.
Prince Edward. Prince = Prints. Edward = Ed-word. Ed + word. He's got a word. Prince Edward. Near enough.
Meghan Markle. Meghan = Meg-an. Markle = Mar-kul. Meg-an at the Mark-et-kul. Meghan Markle. Say it out loud.
Prince William. Prince = Prints. William = Will-yam. Prints + Will-yam. Will's printing. Prince William. Say it out loud.
Queen Camilla. Camilla = Cam-ill-a. Cam (camera) + ill + a. She's got a camera and she's a bit ill about it. Queen Camilla. Near enough.
King George. George = Jaw-j. He's gorging, jaw going. King George. Easy one this.
Princess Anne. Princess = Prin-cess. Anne = Ann. A princess called Ann. Princess Anne. Say it out loud.
King Charles. Charles = Char-lz. Char + ls. He's charring. King Charles. Near enough.
Catherine of Aragon. Aragon = Arr-a-gon. A + gone. She's a-gone. Catherine of Aragon. Say it out loud.
black (dark, no light) + hole (a void, an opening that swallows things) = Black Hole. Spoken aloud: 'black hole', strong and clean split.
neutron (sounds like 'new-tron', or like Newton minus the w) + star (a twinkler in the sky) = Neutron Star. Spoken split: 'new-tron star'.
super (a show-off celebrity, big, flashy) + nova (new, or nova as in a new bright star) = Supernova. Sounds like 'SOO-per-NO-va'.
photo (light, like a photo taken with light) + synthesis (combining things, making something new) = Photosynthesis. Spoken: 'FOE-toe-SIN-thuh-sis'.
mito (sounds like 'my toe') + chon (sounds like 'con') + dria (sounds like 'dree-uh') = Mitochondria. Phonetically: 'MY-toe-KON-dree-uh'.
chroma (colour, from Greek) + somes (sounds like 'some', making it a near-rhyme scheme) = Chromosomes. Spoken: 'KROH-muh-soams'.
anti (against, hostile) + biotics (sounds like 'by-OT-ics', relating to life/biology) = Antibiotics. Spoken: 'AN-tee-by-OT-iks'.
pen (Penn, the magician) + icillin (sounds like 'I-sil-in') = Penicillin. Spoken: 'PEN-ih-SIL-in'. Close enough to 'Penn-ih-sill-in'.
vacci (sounds like 'vax-ee') + nation (a whole country full of people, all queuing up) = Vaccination. Spoken: 'vax-ih-NAY-shun'.
Pasteur (Louis Pasteur, the French scientist) + isation (the process of doing something) = Pasteurisation. Spoken: 'PASS-cher-eye-ZAY-shun'.
radio (radio waves, or the glow/emission) + activity (doing stuff, being active) = Radioactivity. Spoken: 'RAY-dee-oh-ak-TIV-ih-tee'.
nuclear (relating to the atom's nucleus) + fission (splitting, from Latin 'fissio') = Nuclear Fission. Spoken: 'NEW-klee-uh FISH-un'.
quantum (the tiny discrete unit of energy) + mechanics (a person who fixes things, or the study of motion) = Quantum Mechanics. Spoken: 'KWON-tum muh-KAN-iks'.
relative (relating to something else, not absolute) + ity (the state of being) = Relativity. Spoken: 'rel-uh-TIV-ih-tee'.
grave (serious, weighty) + ity (the state of being) = Gravity. Or more directly: 'GRAV-ih-tee', the force that pulls things down.
electro (electricity) + magnet (the thing that sticks to your fridge) + ism (a theory or force) = Electromagnetism. Spoken: 'ih-LEK-troh-MAG-nuh-tiz-um'.
thermo (heat, like a thermometer) + dynamics (movement, energy, laws of motion) = Thermodynamics. Spoken: 'THER-moh-dy-NAM-iks'.
periodic (recurring at intervals, like elements grouped by period) + table (a grid, like a dining table) = Periodic Table. Spoken: 'peer-ee-OD-ik TAY-bul'.
fib (a small lie) + on (continuing) + acci (sounds like 'ah-chee') = Fibonacci. Spoken: 'fib-uh-NAH-chee'. Phonetically: 'FIB-on-AH-chee'.
pie (sounds like 'py', like pi the number) + thag (sounds like 'thag') + oras (sounds like 'oh-ras') = Pythagoras. Spoken: 'py-THAG-uh-rus'.
archi (sounds like 'ark-ee') + medes (sounds like 'mee-deez') = Archimedes. Spoken: 'ark-ih-MEE-deez'. Famous for his bath moment.
pass (to go past something) + cal (sounds like 'kal') = Pascal. Spoken: 'PASS-kul'. Or 'pas-KAL' depending on accent.
boil (to heat liquid until it bubbles) sounds like Boyle. Spoken: 'BOIL' = Boyle. Near enough for a phonetic riddle.
new (brand new) + ton (a unit of weight, heavy) = Newton. Spoken: 'NEW-ton'. Isaac Newton, gravity and that.
DNA (the genetic code) + helix (a spiral shape, like a twisted ladder) = DNA Helix. Spoken: 'dee-en-ay HEE-liks'.
dark (invisible, can't see it) + matter (stuff, the physical substance of things) = Dark Matter. Spoken: 'DARK MAT-er'.
quantum (impossibly small units) + mechanics (a fixer, someone who understands how things work) = Quantum Mechanics.
pie (sounds like 'py') + thag (sounds like 'thag') + oras = Pythagoras. Different clue angle: the running Greek scholar image contrasted with the answer Pythagoras.
super (spectacular, bigger than everything) + nova (new, a new bright light appears) = Supernova. Spoken: 'SOO-per-NOH-vuh'.
electro (electricity, lightning) + magnet (the things that attract) + ism (the unified theory or force) = Electromagnetism.
Pasteur (the French scientist, sounds like 'pass-tur') + isation (the process/suffix) = Pasteurisation. Spoken: 'PASS-chuh-rye-ZAY-shun'.
mito (sounds like 'my toe') + chondria (sounds like 'KON-dree-uh') = Mitochondria. Spoken aloud: 'my-toe-KON-dree-uh'.
periodic (at regular intervals, like the periods in the table) + table (the grid/chart) = Periodic Table.
archi (sounds like 'ark-ee') + medes (sounds like 'mee-deez') = Archimedes. Spoken: 'ARK-ih-MEE-deez'.
vacci (sounds like 'VAKS-ee') + nation (a whole country of people) = Vaccination. Spoken: 'VAK-sih-NAY-shun'.
new (brand new) + ton (a unit of weight, very heavy) = Newton. Spoken: 'NEW-tun'. Isaac Newton, the unit of force.
black (no light, dark) + hole (a void, an opening you fall into) = Black Hole. Spoken: 'BLAK HOHL'. Very clean split.
radio (emissions, waves, energy going outward) + activity (doing things, being busy and active) = Radioactivity.
fib (a small lie, or the start of the name) + on-acci (sounds like 'on-AH-chee') = Fibonacci. Spoken: 'FIB-uh-NAH-chee'.
chroma (colour, Greek for pigment - chromosomes were named because they took up coloured dye) + some (a body/package, Greek 'soma') = Chromosomes. Spoken: 'KROH-muh-sohmz'.
grave (serious, heavy - 'gravitas' is Latin for weight) + ity (the state of being) = Gravity. Spoken: 'GRAV-ih-tee'.
nuclear (of the nucleus, the atom's core) + fission (the splitting, from 'fissio') = Nuclear Fission. Spoken: 'NEW-klee-uh FISH-un'.
pen (sounds like 'pen') + ih + cillin (sounds like 'SIL-in') = Penicillin. Spoken: 'PEN-ih-SIL-in'. Fleming found it in the mould.
Pasteur (Louis Pasteur, French microbiologist) + isation (the process of applying something) = Pasteurisation.
thermo (heat, Greek 'thermos') + dynamics (the laws of energy and motion) = Thermodynamics. Spoken: 'THER-moh-dy-NAM-iks'.
dark (invisible, no light interaction) + matter (physical stuff, mass) = Dark Matter. Spoken: 'DARK MAT-er'. Very clean split.
anti (against, hostile to the enemy) + biotics (life-related, from 'bios') = Antibiotics. Spoken: 'AN-tee-by-OT-iks'.
boil (what water does when heated - sounds exactly like Boyle) = Boyle. Spoken: 'BOYL'. Robert Boyle, gas laws.
pass (to move past, or 'pas' in French) + cal (sounds like 'kul') = Pascal. Spoken: 'PASS-kul'. Blaise Pascal, mathematician.
neutron (the uncharged particle in the nucleus) + star (a massive stellar object) = Neutron Star. Spoken: 'NEW-tron STAR'.
DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid, the genetic molecule) + helix (spiral shape, the double helix structure) = DNA Helix.
photo (light) + synthesis (the combining process, making something new from parts) = Photosynthesis. Spoken: 'FOH-toh-SIN-thuh-sis'.
relative (depending on the observer, not fixed) + ity (the quality/state) = Relativity. Spoken: 'rel-uh-TIV-ih-tee'.
fib (sounds like the start) + on (continuing sound) + acci (sounds like 'AH-chee') = Fibonacci. Spoken: 'FIB-uh-NAH-chee'.
py (sounds like 'pie', the circular number) + thagoras (sounds like 'THAG-uh-rus') = Pythagoras. Spoken: 'py-THAG-uh-rus'.
quantum (the smallest unit of energy, very tiny scale) + mechanics (engineering, the rules of how things work) = Quantum Mechanics.
new (brand new discovery) + ton (heavy, the unit of weight) = Newton. Spoken: 'NEW-tun'. Sir Isaac Newton.
electro (electricity, from Maxwell's equations) + magnet (the attractive force) + ism (the unified theory) = Electromagnetism.
thermo (heat, temperature - from 'thermos', Greek for hot) + dynamics (laws of motion and energy) = Thermodynamics.
grave (serious, weighty - Latin 'gravitas' means weight) + ity (the state of being) = Gravity. Spoken: 'GRAV-ih-tee'.
Hurri (her-ee) + cane (walking stick) = Hurricane. Her + cane = Hurricane.
Fog sounds like fog. Clue built around the concept of Frank + og... actually fog = f + og. Just means you can't see. Phonetic scene: fog = can't see Frank.
Rain (Ray-n) + bow (bow and arrow) = Rainbow. Ray sounds like rain, bow is the archer's tool.

Thunder (thun-der) + storm = Thunderstorm. Thun sounds like Tom + drum? Actually: thunder = thun-der, storm = storm. Tom + drum + storm? Better: thun-der-storm. The clue: 'thunder' sounds like 'Tom's drum'... near enough.
Light (Len's brightness) + ning = Lightning. Light + ning = Lightning. Clue built around something bright and fast.
Slee (slipper-ish sound) + t = Sleet. Slee sounds like 'slee' from slippers, t at the end. Near enough.
Bliz (Billie's + z sound) + zard (wizard) = Blizzard. Billy + wizard = Blizzard. Near enough when said aloud.
Heat (dead hot) + wave (Wayne = wave) = Heatwave. Heat + wave = Heatwave. Wave sounds like Wayne near enough.
Driz (drip + iz) + zle = Drizzle. Sounds like a dribbling drip. Drizzle = light annoying rain. Scene works phonetically.
Tor (tore) + nado (Ned-o) = Tornado. Tore + Ned + o = Tornado. Said aloud: tor-NAY-do sounds like tore-Ned-oh. Near enough.
Frost = frost. Scene describes frost on the ground. F + rost. Phonetically direct.
Hail (them stones from the sky) + stone = Hailstone. Hail + stone = Hailstone. Dead straightforward that.
Snow (white cold stuff) + flake (a single flat piece) = Snowflake. Snow + flake = Snowflake. Scene describes a snowflake directly.
Mon (Mon) + soon (spoon minus the p) = Monsoon. Mon + soon = Monsoon. Soon sounds like spoon said lazy. Near enough.
Cycle (cycling round) + one (lone) = Cyclone. Cy + clone... or cycle + own = Cyclone. Said aloud: SY-clone = cycle + own. Works.
Ty (fella called Ty) + phoon (fun said lazy = phoon) = Typhoon. Ty + phoon = Typhoon. Ty + foon sounds like Typhoon aloud.
Ava (name Ava) + launch (a rocket launch) = Avalanche. Ava + launch = Avalanche. Said aloud: AV-a-launch = Avalanche. Near enough.
El (little fella called El) + Nino (like a little one, nino = child in Spanish) = El Nino. El + nee-nyo = El Nino. Scene: warm boy called El.
Jet (a fast jet) + stream (a river/stream) = Jet Stream. Jet + stream = Jet Stream. Dead literal that one.
Baro (Barry-oh) + meter (a measuring device) = Barometer. Barry + meter = Barometer. Baro sounds like Barry said quick. Near enough.
Iso (equal, like isolated) + bar (a bar you go to) = Isobar. Eye-so + bar = Isobar. The bar scene works phonetically.
Cirrus = SIR-us. Sir (like a knight, sir) + us = Cirrus. Sir + us = Cirrus. The clue: someone posh up high. Near enough.
Stratus = STRAT-us. Strat (flat layer) + us = Stratus. Sounds like stratum + us. Flat layer cloud.
Cumulo (accumulated, piling up) + nimbus (nimble bus?) = Cumulonimbus. Kyoom-you-lo + nim-bus = Cumulonimbus. The nimble bus climbing up to the cumulus. Bit of a stretch but sounds right aloud.
Nimbo (nimble + o) + stratus (flat cloud layer) = Nimbostratus. Nim-bo + stra-tus = Nimbostratus. Nimble bus on a flat road covered in grey.
Anem (Anim... Anem sounds like 'a name') + ometer (a meter) = Anemometer. An-ee-mom-eter. An (a) + nemo (like the fish) + meter = Anemometer. A Nemo with a meter. That works aloud.
Cold (Colin's gone cold) + front (front of the queue) = Cold Front. Cold + front = Cold Front. Very literal, that one.
Super (proper super) + cell (a cell) = Supercell. Super + cell = Supercell. Dead literal phonetically.
Ha (ha!) + boob... actually Haboob = ha-BOOB. Ha + boo + b. Or: Ha + Bob = Haboob. Fella called Bob who goes ha = Haboob. Said aloud it works.
Hail (hail weather) + stone (one stone) = Hailstone. Hail + stone = Hailstone.
Frost = frost. F + rost. Roy + ost? No, just frost. Scene describes frost on the windscreen. Phonetically direct.
Drizzle = DRIZ-zul. Sounds like a dribble crossed with a mizzle. Scene: annoying non-rain on your face.
Sleet = sleet. Sl + eet. Slee + t. Slippery + eat? Scene describes sleet: wet cold mixture. Phonetically direct.
Rain (after the rain) + bow (like an arc or bow shape) = Rainbow. Rain + bow = Rainbow.
Tor (tore, as in spinning fast) + nado (Tom + ado = nado) = Tornado. Torn + ado = Tornado. Said aloud: tor-NAY-do = tore + Ned + oh.
Hurri (her-ee) + cane (walking stick) = Hurricane. Her + cane = Hurricane.
Bliz (Billy's + z) + zard (wizard without the wi) = Blizzard. Billy + wizard = Blizzard. Said aloud: BLIZ-ard = Billy's wizard. Near enough.
Light (bright flash) + ning = Lightning. Light + ning = Lightning. Len the bright flasher.
Mon (fella called Mon) + soon (spoon without the p, said lazily) = Monsoon. Mon + soon = Monsoon.

Jet (aircraft jet) + stream (a flowing stream) = Jet Stream. Jet + stream = Jet Stream. Very literal.
Cycle (cycling round) + one = Cyclone. Cy + clone. Said aloud: SY-clone = cycle + own. Spinning bike of weather.
Ty (fella called Ty) + phoon (like buffoon without the buf) = Typhoon. Ty + foon = Typhoon. Said aloud it works.
Heat (massive warmth) + wave (Wayne = wave, Wayne's bringing waves) = Heatwave. Heat + wave = Heatwave.
Stratus = STRAT-us. Strat (flat strategy/layer) + us = Stratus. Flat cloud, flat strategy. Layer upon layer.
Iso (equal, isolated) + bar (a bar venue) = Isobar. Eye-so + bar = Isobar. Equal pressure bar.
Ava (name Ava) + launch (launching things) = Avalanche. Ava + launch = Avalanche. AV-a-launch = Avalanche aloud.
Nimbo (nimble + o) + stratus (flat layer cloud) = Nimbostratus. Nim-bo + stra-tus = Nimbostratus. Nimble flat grey thing.

Baro (Barry-oh said quick) + meter (measuring gauge) = Barometer. Barry + meter = Barometer. Bar-o-meter.

El (little boy El) + Nino (nino = little one, also sounds like 'knee-no') = El Nino. El + nee-nyo = El Nino. The warm little boy.
Ha (ha!) + Bob (fella called Bob) = Haboob. Ha + boo + b = Haboob. Ha-Bob said quick = Haboob aloud.
An (a) + nemo (Nemo the fish, or nee-mo) + meter (measuring device) = Anemometer. A + Nemo + meter = Anemometer. Said aloud: an-ee-MOM-eter. A Nemo with a meter.
Cold (Colin's gone cold) + front (pushed to the front) = Cold Front. Cold + front = Cold Front. Very direct.
Snow (the cold white stuff) + flake (a single thin piece) = Snowflake. Snow + flake = Snowflake. Direct phonetic match.
Thunder (drum crash sound) + storm (the whole event) = Thunderstorm. Thunder + storm = Thunderstorm. Tom's drum storm.

moan (to complain) + a (the sound between) + leaser (someone who hires things out, a lease-er) = Mona Lisa. Moan-a-Leaser.

star-ry (covered in stars, like space) + knit (knitting) → star-ree-nite → starry night. Star-ee + night (nite sounds like knit said fast). Actually: star + ry sounds like starry, and knit sounds like night said casually = Starry Night.
the + scream (a massive yell/shout) = The Scream. Exactly what it says.
gurn (to pull a grotesque face, very common British phrase) + icker (slang for underwear/knickers, said fast) = gurn-icker = Guernica. Say it out loud: gurn-icker.
son (your boy) + flowers (plants) = son-flowers = Sunflowers. Son + flowers said together.
wart (a small skin growth) + er (the sound) + Lil (a woman's name) + ees = wart-er-Lil-ees = Water Lilies. Warter-Lilees.
girl (a woman) + with (with) + a (a) + pearl (a gemstone from an oyster) + earring (something stuck to the ear) = Girl with a Pearl Earring. The clue describes the painting directly through sound.
the + birth (arrival/emergence) + of + Venus (the planet/goddess) = The Birth of Venus. Birth = arrival, Venus = the planet named after the goddess.
the + last (final) + supper (an evening meal) = The Last Supper. The clue describes the scene: final meal, long table, things went wrong after.
cube (a box shape) + ism (an artistic movement/ideology) = Cubism. Cube + ism said together.
sir (a knight, a title) + real (genuine, actual) + ism (a movement/belief) = Sir-real-ism = Surrealism. Say it: sur-real-ism.
pop (informal word for dad/father) + art (painting/artwork) = Pop Art. Pop + Art said together.

Fran (short for Frances/Francis) + sis (a suffix sound) + bay (a coastal inlet) + con (sounds like 'con') ... actually: Francis sounds like Fran-sis, Bacon = bay-con. Fran-sis + bay-con = Francis Bacon.
L (the letter L) + S (the letter S) + low (not high) + ree (a sound/name) = L.S. Low-ree = L.S. Lowry. The initials plus Low+ree.
John (a man's name, Jon) + con (to deceive/trick) + stable (where you keep large animals like cows and horses) = John Con-stable = John Constable. Jon + con + stable.
camp (a bit theatrical/eccentric) + bell (a ringing thing, church bell) + s (possessive) + soup (the tinned stuff) + cans (the tins) = Campbell's Soup Cans. Camp + bell + s + soup + cans.
the + birth (emergence/appearing) + of + Venus (the goddess/planet) = The Birth of Venus.
moan (to moan/complain) + a (the sound) + leaser (someone who leases/rents things) = Mona-Lisa. Moan-a-Lease-er.
sur (sounds like 'sir') + real (genuine/actual) + ism = sur-real-ism = Surrealism. Not convinced anything is real.
im-press (to press something with an iron, leaving a mark/impression) + ion + ism = Impressionism.
bank (savings institution, where you keep money) + sky/see (to look at/observe) = bank-see = Banksy.
gurn (to pull an exaggerated face) + icker (slang for knickers/underwear) = gurn-icker = Guernica.
cube (a geometric solid, a box shape) + ism (a movement or ideology) = Cubism.
Francis (a name, Fran-sis) + Bacon (cured pork, the stuff you have with eggs) = Francis Bacon.
William (Will + i + am) + hog (a pig) + arth (a strangled gargling throat sound, like 'argh') = William Hogarth. Will-yam-hog-arth.
John (the man's name) + con (to trick or deceive) + stable (where horses live) = John-con-stable = John Constable.
L (letter L) + S (letter S) + low (close to the ground, not high) + ry/ree (a sound like 'ree') = L.S. Low-ree = L.S. Lowry.
the + scream (a loud shout or yell, which is exactly what the painting depicts) = The Scream.
bank (a building society/financial institution) + see (to look at, 'sky' said casually sounds like 'see') = bank-see = Banksy. Also bank + sky.
ab (stomach muscle) + stract (pulled/stretched, abstract) + express (fast non-stop train) + ion + ism = abstract-expressionism.
